**"Then maybe..."** Marina looks at the dormant droid closest to them, one of the three standing sentinel before the crystal. **"...maybe we don't need to power the lock. Maybe we need to access the system through one of *them*."**

Keeping his weapon trained on his three targets he turned his head to look at Marina and Sera who were talking tech.  He wasn’t tech oriented like those two but it sounded like Marina’s idea was to hack the system *through* one of the droids.  That raised some questions.  


**”Hack the system through the droids?    Wouldn’t that set the droids off and activate them.”**

Marina turns her head, her helmeted gaze meeting Wes's. **"Normally, yes. A direct, aggressive hack like Sera did on the terminal would almost certainly trigger a defensive response. But..."** She hesitates, then looks meaningfully at Wes, her voice dropping slightly, becoming more personal. **"...what you did with the Wayfinder... that wasn't an aggressive hack. You *soothed* it. You connected with it through the Force, calmed its energy, and it *responded* to you."**

She gestures from the dormant droids back to the pulsating crystal. **"Sera said these droids are terminals, extensions of the main system, and that whole system is powered by... *that*,"** she indicates the massive, Force-resonant crystal. **"They're not just machines, Wes. They're conduits for the same kind of ancient, Force-attuned energy we felt in that cylinder. Maybe they won't respond to a standard slicing attempt, but... they might respond to *you*."**

Sera, catching the gist of Marina's idea, looks from the droids to Wes, her expression a mixture of professional skepticism and intense curiosity. **"Whoa, hang on,"** she interjects. **"You want him to... what? Meditate at a killer janitor-bot? Marina, their security subroutine is probably hardwired. The moment a non-designated user—that's us—tries to access their core programming, they're designed to activate and neutralize the threat."** She shakes her head. **"My tools work on code. I can't slice a 'vibe'. But..."** She looks at Wes, a calculating glint in her eye. **"...if he *could* use the Force to somehow put the droid's core programming into a passive, receptive state... a diagnostic mode, maybe... it might lower the firewalls enough for me to slip in and access the cargo release protocols without triggering the 'decontaminate everything' response. It's a crazy long shot, but it's the only plan we've got that doesn't involve cutting into a live power conduit or praying we can outrun these things."**

Wes felt the hesitation in Marina’s voice.  Then he heard and felt it shift to a more personal level as she continued over the team's comms.  She wasn’t wrong while he didn’t actually converse with the Wayfinder he did communicate with it.  It was solely through the Force and if he hadn’t the fear was that it would have had a catastrophic event of some sort.   


As Sera spoke he turned his gaze from Marina and took in both the giant crystal and the droids.   Both were comparatively huge in comparison to what the younglings found.  


He let out a breath that they all heard.  **”I never would have expected the Wayfinder to react to me the way it did.  And now I can say that the vision I had before the mission now makes more sense.   I couldn’t draw a line from the Wayfinder to the *Veiled Shadow* but perhaps this is it.   One ancient attuned Force artifact to another.”**  He stopped there before saying that it was because of the Force that he was here on this mission.  His interaction with the Wayfinder and now here.  The coincidence was simply too much.  


He looked back at Marina and gave her a reassuring smile.  He was glad she was here.  He knew without a shred of doubt she would be here to help him in a way the others couldn’t.  He gave them all a nod.  **”Alrignt then. Let’s go have a talk with the droids.”**  He pointed at Roric and Echo. **”Establish vantage points that covers us”**  He handed the Ion rifle over to Echo.  **”If this goes south and they decide to ‘clean’ me,”**  He said with a combination of humor and seriousness. **”Shoot it.”**  He still had an Ion grenade so he’d be able to do something at  ground zero if he had to.  


He looked at Echo.  **”Do you need to be here at the container or at the droid to do your thing once I’ve done mine?”**

Sera processes Wes's plan and the gravity of the situation, her expression shifting to one of intense professional focus. **"To get into their cargo control systems, I'll need a direct physical interface,"** she answers, pulling a specialized, multi-pronged data spike from her kit. **"That means I need to be right at the droid's primary access port while you're... doing your thing. I'll stay behind you, ready to plug in the instant you give the signal that it's in a receptive state. If this works, I should be able to access the whole cargo network from that one droid, including the lock on 28-C."** She gives the data spike a confident spin. **"Just try not to get 'cleaned' before I get the door open, alright?"**

**"Copy that, Wes,"** Echo confirms, accepting the ion rifle. She hefts it, checking the power cell with practiced efficiency. **"I'll find a high-ground position on these containers. It'll give me a clear line of sight on you, the droid, and the surrounding area. If it wakes up on the wrong side of the bed, I'll have a shot."** Without another word, she moves with silent grace, using the shadows and the colossal containers to find an elevated, defensible overwatch position.

**"Understood,"** Roric grunts, already moving to a different angle, ensuring he has a clear field of fire that complements Echo's, covering the other dormant droids and the corridor they came from. **"Rear and flank security is set. We're ready."**

The team repositions with quiet efficiency. Echo disappears into the shadows atop a massive container, her rifle a faint silhouette against the pulsating blue light. Roric becomes a steadfast guardian, covering their escape route. Sera moves up behind Wes, her data spike held ready, her eyes fixed on the target droid.

The stage is set. In the center of the vast, silent bay, Wes and Marina now stand before one of the three insectoid droids, its inert form bathed in the rhythmic glow of the colossal crystal. The air is thick with anticipation and the palpable, ancient energy of the chamber.

Marina moves to stand beside Wes, close enough for their shoulders to almost touch. She doesn't speak, but her presence is a tangible support. She gives him a small, reassuring nod, visible in the glow of the crystal. Closing her eyes behind her own visor, she extends her senses, not towards the droid itself, but towards the ambient Force energy flowing between the crystal and the machine, seeking to find the subtle 'current' or 'frequency' of its dormant state to help guide Wes's own effort. The air grows thick with unspoken tension.

In the vast emptiness of the cargo hold they were all in place.   Echo and Roric had taken up positions to provide cover in case this went sideways.  Sera was behind him ready to spring into action with a physical interface.  Her asking him to hit get ‘cleaned’ brought a small smile and a nod in her direction.  


Perhaps the most reassuring presence was Marina’s.  She was ever watchful and supportive.   Standing in the glow of the crystal he returned the nod as he  reached out to her thankful for her reassuring presence.  


He felt her reach out towards the crystal as he turned his attention to the massive droid that was before the three of them.  Like the wayfinder he figured that he would need a more hands on feeling that allowed for a more physical as well spiritual connection through the Force.  **”Moving closer.  I’ll update as much as I can.”**  He said to the group as he took two steps and placed a hand gently, gingerly upon the cold metal housing that encapsulated the droid’s innards.  


Closing his eyes and taking a deep breath he opened himself up to the Force. He took  in the massive hanger bay before narrowing his focus on the object of his attention, the droid and in essence the massive crystal that it was connected to.  ***”Hello.”***  He thought.

As Wes takes the final steps and places his hand on the cold, ancient metal of the dormant droid, Marina moves up beside him, her gloved hand coming to rest gently on his armored forearm. A subtle current of calm, focused energy flows from her, not to the droid, but to Wes, reinforcing their bond, a silent anchor amidst the overwhelming Force resonance of the chamber.

For a heart-stopping moment, there is nothing but the rhythmic pulse of the crystal's light. Then, a profound change occurs. The dormant void he had sensed within the droid doesn't flare to hostile life, but instead... softens. The cold emptiness is replaced by a faint, neutral hum in the Force. A series of soft, pale-blue indicator lights, previously dark, flicker to life along the droid's insectoid chassis, glowing with a steady, non-threatening light. A low, mechanical whir emanates from within, the sound of ancient systems waking from a long slumber into a passive, diagnostic state. The droid remains perfectly still, its weaponized appendages inert, but it is no longer just a cold machine. It is... listening. Receptive.

Sera, who had been holding her breath, her data spike poised, sees the lights and hears the hum. **"That's it, Wes, that's the signal!"** she whispers urgently over the comm. **"It's in a passive state! I've got a window! Moving in!"**

With practiced speed, Sera darts forward, kneeling beside the now-glowing droid. She finds the primary interface port and, with a firm, decisive motion, plunges her data spike into the connection. Her datapad flares to life, lines of archaic code scrolling at an impossible speed.

**"I'm in!"** she reports, her voice a triumphant hiss. **"Bypassing primary security... accessing root directories... I have the cargo manifests... and... I have the release protocols for the pressure-sealed containers! Sending the sequence for 28-C to your datapad now, Marina!"**

Just before placing his hand in the droid he felt Marina’s hand on his armored forearm.  He could feel the cool resolve of her presence as she helped reinforce the bond they shared.  It was a welcome anchor in the vastness of the cargo hold that was flooded by the calm yet overwhelming Force resonance of the cargo bay. 


A long heartbeat after reaching out, it all changed.  The droid flared to life.  Not as the killing machine they were all expecting but as a passive neutral resonance within the Force.  It was the exact opposite of what he had come to know of normal droids who were dead spaces, voids in the Force.  


Opening his eyes he saw the pale blue lights become active along the chassis.  And a slight vibration as its internals also came to life within.  It was now active.  Listening and he felt that it was receptive as he heard the excited exclamation from Sera as she went to work.   **”Go for it.  All I’ve said effectively was ‘hello’.”**


As Sera went to work he remained calm as he felt the gentle thrum of the droid's presence and the greater presence of the massive crystal that wasn't much farther away.    When he heard that Sera had the sequence for the cargo container he nodded.   **”Go.  Open it up.  I’ll handle it here.”**  


He kept his connection with the droid open looking for anything familiar like before with the Wayfinder.   ***”What can you tell me?”***  He thought as remembered the brief glimpse of the starfield the Wayfinder had shown him.

**"Copy that, Sera. Sequence received,"** Marina confirms, her voice tight with focus. She moves swiftly but carefully toward the massive Container 28-C, her datapad already displaying the complex release protocols. **"Wes, I'm at the container lock. Roric, Echo, be ready."** She begins inputting the commands, her fingers flying across the screen.

As Marina works on the container, Wes maintains his delicate Force-link with the now-passive droid. He keeps his hand resting on its cool, metallic chassis, projecting calm and inquisitive intent. ***"What can you tell me?"*** he thinks, focusing his will, remembering the fleeting star map from the Wayfinder.

The droid, being a mere terminal and not a repository of cosmic knowledge like the Wayfinder, does not respond with visions of nebulae or ancient pathways. Instead, as a direct result of Wes's focused Force query on its now-receptive systems, a different kind of information flows.

The pale blue lights on its chassis flicker in a complex, rhythmic pattern, and a low, harmonic tone emits from its internal vocalizer. Simultaneously, a stream of data floods Sera's active connection.

**"Whoa—Wes, whatever you just did, it's... it's accessing something,"** Sera's voice crackles with surprise. **"It's... a log. A ship's log. Very old, very fragmented."** On the shared HUD, a few lines of translated, archaic text begin to appear, pulled from the data stream:

`...Log Entry 7.3.1... Anomaly detected... Karthon Expanse...`
`...gravitic shear extreme... hull integrity failing...`
`...the PATHFINDER's signal is gone... lost in the whisper...`
`...containment field for the HEART is fluctuating... automated reclamation protocols engaged...`
`...May the Makers guide us... Log ends...`

Just as the final fragment appears, a loud series of **CLUNKS** echoes from Container 28-C as Marina successfully inputs the final release sequence. The massive mechanical locks disengage, and the main door begins to hiss open, revealing the dark, silent interior.

The droid under Wes's hand goes silent, its lights dimming back to a soft, steady glow, the data stream cut off as if its brief, primary function—responding to the query—is complete. It remains passive, 

The team's suit lights immediately cut into the darkness of the container. Inside, secured in ancient, dusty stasis racks, are several large, cylindrical objects. They are unmistakably Hyperdrive Energizer Coils, their design archaic but their condition, at first glance, remarkably well-preserved. They have found Gaxx's primary objective.

Wes was at odds with what to do.  They had the door to the container open and the primary objective was now in hand.  He also had a way to communicate with the ship.   Like the Wayfinder it was responding to him though the Force.   He had a chance to find out more but they had other objectives to find as well.  He kept his connection with the droid or the ship as he wasn’t sure which he was communicating with.  **”Sera, Marina did the manifest we found give us a location of the other two objectives?   The Nav Matrix Stabilizers and the High-Yield Power Converters?  We also need to get these Energizer coils back.   We’re going to need a load lifter or two.”**


He paused for a second.  **”Does anyone have a mission related question they want me to ask the ship?”**

Marina, her attention now fully on the open container, activates her suit lights to peer inside. **"Wes, the manifest confirms the 'Star-Leap' Energizer Coils are in here, secured in containment racks,"** she reports, her voice filled with professional satisfaction. She then consults her datapad, cross-referencing the data Sera had unlocked. **"According to this, the Nav-Matrix Stabilizers are located in a secure vault adjacent to the main bridge, Deck 2. The High-Yield Power Converters are in the primary engineering section, one deck below us on Sub-level 4. Both are... considerable distances from here."**

She then addresses his other point, her light sweeping the vast, dark hangar bay outside their immediate area. **"As for transport, the manifest lists several 'Class-4 Cargo Lifter Droids' assigned to this bay. The schematics show their charging alcoves are on the far side, near the main hangar doors. If they're still functional, that's our best bet for moving these coils."**

Sera, still connected to the passive droid, considers Wes's question about asking the ship something. **"Good question,"** she muses. **"If it's responding to you, ask it for the direct operational status of those Cargo Lifter Droids. If it can tell us if they're functional, or if their power cells are completely dead, it could save us a long walk across this bay for nothing."** She looks up from her datapad. **"Also, ask it for the security status between here and the bridge. Knowing the exact locations of any other 'janitor' droids along Marina's plotted route would be... extremely helpful."**

Wes smiled as they asked questions.   He nodded his head as he spoke.  **”Do you think the load lifters could be given orders and sent back to the docking bay on their own?”**


As they figured out the answer to his question he looked back to the droid.  His connection with it and the ship still in place.  Working through that connection he worked with mental images and words.   ***”Are the load lifters operational?”*** He didn’t know what the ones Marina mentioned looked like so he used what he knew as he continued on.  ***”Whats the operational security on the way to the bridge and primary engineering on Sub-level 4?  Is there a way to travel to both quickly?”***  He used images of the maintenance droids and elevators to help with the context of the questions.

**"Automated return?"** Marina considers Wes's question, her practical mind immediately assessing the variables. **"Possible, if they have a basic pathfinding AI and we can designate the *Void Skimmer* as a 'delivery point' in their command protocols. But given the state of this ship, I wouldn't count on it. We should probably plan on escorting them back."**

As Wes focuses his will, channeling his questions through the passive droid, the response is immediate and surprisingly clear. His critical success with the Force forges a deeper, more lucid connection, not just with the droid, but with the ship's core operational memory itself.

The pale blue lights on the droid's chassis pulse in a complex, rapid sequence. On Sera's datapad, and a moment later on the team's HUDs, new information streams in, clearer and more direct than the fragmented log entry.

A simplified schematic of the hangar bay appears, highlighting three specific alcoves on the far side. Two of the alcoves glow with a steady, faint green light. The third is a dull, inert red.

`// Class-4 Cargo Lifters // Status: Unit 02, Unit 04 - Functional, Standby Power. Unit 03 - Offline, Power Cell Depleted. Pathfinding AI: Intact. Remote Designation Protocol: Available. //`

Immediately after, two new paths illuminate on their main schematic overlay. The first, a route to the bridge on Deck 2, is marked with several red icons—the 'silent guardian' droids—at key junctions.

`// Security Status: Bridge Route. Alert: Multiple H.C.R.U. units dormant in corridors 12-A, 8-F, and Bridge Access Hall. Threat Condition: Dormant but Linked to Primary Security Grid. //`

The second path, leading down to primary engineering on Sub-level 4, appears clear of any red droid icons. However, a different kind of alert flashes.

`// Security Status: Engineering Route. No H.C.R.U. units detected. Warning: Structural Integrity Compromised in Sector 4-G. Atmospheric Seal Failure Detected. Proceed with Extreme Caution. //`

Finally, a new detail appears on their map: a series of smaller, previously hidden lines are highlighted, representing service corridors and maintenance accessways. One of these lines shows a direct, albeit likely cramped, route from a point near the turbolift they arrived on, leading almost directly to the bridge access hall, bypassing two of the droid-patrolled main corridors.

`// Alternate Route Designation: Maintenance Corridor 7. Status: Clear. Access: Restricted (Manual Override Required at Entry Point). //`

Sera lets out a low whistle as the data floods her screen. **"Wes... it's not just answering. It's giving us real-time status and tactical overlays. Two of those cargo lifters are functional, and it looks like we can program them to return to the *Skimmer*. And... it just showed us a back way to the bridge that bypasses most of those dormant droids."** She looks from her screen to the passive droid Wes is touching, her expression one of pure, unadulterated awe. **"You're not just talking to a droid. You're talking to the *ship*."**

Wes felt the shift with the droid and possibly the ship itself.   He let his connection open freely and he felt, if he wasn’t imagining it, the ship open up as well.  


His questions seemed to be received with clear and conscious meaning because immediately information was rolling in.  As they all read the scrolling information and new pathways that the ship showed them Sera was confirming that he seemed to be talking to the ship now, not just the droid.   


**”Copy that Sera.  I felt a shift when I pushed a little deeper.   I seem to have forged a clear Force connection to the *ship*.  This reminds me of the Wayfinder we found on the Chu’unthor.”**  He paused for a second as he thought of the ancient Force sensitive relic.  **”See if we can get those load lifters moving.   We’ve just been given a gift. Let’s not waste it.   We’ve got clearly defined designated pathways to the Bridge and Sub-level 4.  Let’s figure out the most time efficient way to hit them up.   Marina, is there anything else here that may be of use to the Chu’unthor?”**


As they went to work he once again began talking to the ship.  He wanted to let Kael know that there would be incoming load lifters and to secure the cargo.  Again he reached out his focus on the still clear connection.  ***”Are there internship comms we can use?”***  


He paused as he considered.  What he really wanted to ask.  The dreams since coming in contact with the Wayfinder seemed more relevant now.   This seemed to be the best chance at finding out more.   He opened up the private channel he had with Marina.  **”Cyar’ika, I’m going to ask the ship about the Wayfinder and the map.  This is all too coincidental.  The Wayfinder, this ship and the connection they both have within the Force.”**

He once again focused his will and concentration on the connection with the ship.   Images of the map and the Wayfinder he had interacted with not so long ago.  The one that had been giving him nightly dreams.   ***”What do you know of this map and what we call a ‘Wayfinder’?  Is it the same as the ‘pathfinder’ you mentioned earlier?”***

Marina, having absorbed the tactical data dump with a mixture of professional focus and quiet awe, looks up from her datapad as Wes lays out the next steps. **"Copy that, Wes. The manifest is massive, but I can run a targeted search for any other high-priority components listed in Gaxx's secondary logs – things like atmospheric processor filters or medical stasis components – that might be in nearby containers. It'll take a few minutes to cross-reference the storage codes."**

She hears his private comm, her expression turning serious as he voices his intent to ask about the Wayfinder. Through their bond, she can feel his conviction, the undeniable sense of converging fate. She gives a small, almost imperceptible nod of support, her hand tightening reassuringly on the tool she's holding. **"Be careful, Wes,"** she transmits back, her voice a low murmur. **"We don't know what kind of answer you'll get."**

As Wes poses his questions to the ship through the Force-attuned droid, the response is twofold.

First, in answer to the practical query about internal comms, a soft chime emanates from a panel on the wall near the turbolift. A single, pale blue light begins to pulse steadily beside what looks like an ancient comms speaker grille.

`// Internal Ship Communications // Status: Localized Power Available. Network Integrity: 27%. Channel: Engineering Sub-level 3. //`

The ship has activated a local comms unit for them.

Then, as Wes pushes his more profound question—about the Wayfinder, the map, the connection—the response is not data, but a powerful, direct sensation through the Force that floods Wes's senses and, to a lesser, more muted degree, Marina's.

It feels like a deep, sonorous chord of **Recognition**. The ambient Force resonance in the chamber intensifies for a moment, and the colossal crystal pulses with a brighter, more insistent sapphire light. Wes is flooded with a powerful impression, clearer than any of his dreams: the image of the *Veiled Shadow* not as a derelict, but as a vibrant, living vessel, and at its heart, a memory of the Wayfinder he held. The two are not the same, but they are intrinsically linked. They feel like a **lock and key**. The Wayfinder is the key, the *Veiled Shadow* is the lock, and the star map he saw was a glimpse of the pathway *beyond* the lock. The feeling is overwhelming—that this entire vessel is a colossal, ancient machine, and the Wayfinder is the missing component required for its true activation. Simultaneously, a new, single line of translated text appears on Sera's datapad, a direct, almost poetic response to his query:

`// The Key seeks its lock. The Path awaits the Pathfinder. //`

**"Wes? Marina?"** Sera's voice cuts through the profound Force-impression, laced with concern. **"Your vitals just spiked on my suit monitor. And... I'm getting a new text string from the droid. It doesn't look like a standard log entry. What's happening?"**

Wes heard the soft chime.  It was an answer to his question about ship comms.  He wasn’t sure if that meant they could contact Kael in the hanger bay but they could figure that out after.   


When he changed thoughts to the map and the Wayfinder the response he received back wasn’t what he was prepared for.  The sensations through the Force overtook his senses.  Through the bond with Marina he sensed she also felt it to some degree.  He wondered if she was seeing what he did.   He wasn’t sure if it was her own Force sensitivity or the bond that allowed it to happen.  Either way his senses were flooded and he fought to reign them in.


Recognition washed over him as he saw the *Veiled Shadow* as it once was in its prime.   With his eyes closed he didn’t see the vibrant sapphire light shine brighter, more insistent from the colossal crystal.  He didn’t need to though as he felt it pulse within the Force as the impressions were clearer than his previous dreams.  


What he did know was that the ship recognized the Wayfinder.   How he didn’t know but it did.   The *Veiled Shadow*, the Wayfinder and the map were all intrinsically linked.  They had the key back on the *Chu’unthor*.  The *Veiled Shadow* was the lock.  And the map was somehow the pathway after the lock.  He shuffled his feet slightly to make sure he had good footing.  The sensation was overwhelming and he wondered if Marina was seeing it all as well.  


They *have* the key to the *Veiled Shadow.*  This ancient ship that they were on.   That realization hit hard as Sera’s voice came across the comms.   He could hear the concern in her voice as she mentioned their vitals and the incoming text.  When he read it, it looked more like an actual response.  Almost sentient like.

**”Sera, I was asking it about something that was found on the *Chu’unthor*.”**  He took a deep breath to calm his vitals as he started a Jedi calming ritual.   **”I suspected that it was linked to this ship and I was right.  I can’t even explain the implications or importance right now.”**  He turned his head to where Marina was.   **”Marina, did you see and feel what I did?”**  He paused for a second as he thought about his next question.  **”Marina,”** He said with incredulity in his voice not believing he was about to ask this.  **”That statement the ship just said. ‘The key seeks its lock.  The path awaits its pathfinder’”**  Hair rose on the back of his neck and he knew Marina could sense his emotions.   


**”Rin,”**  He used the name he only used when in private with her.  It simply emphasized the seriousness of his next words over the teams comms.  **”If the *Veiled Shadow* is the lock, the Wayfinder on the *Chu’unthor* is the key and the path is the map.”**  His pause hung heavy in the silence.  **”Am I maybe the pathfinder?”**

Marina stumbles a half-step, her free hand instinctively flying to her temple as the overwhelming Force-impression from the ship washes over Wes and bleeds through their bond. She feels a wave of vertigo, a chaotic jumble of sensations – the cold vastness of space, the deep thrum of ancient purpose, the heartbreaking sorrow of a lost connection. It’s a powerful, disorienting echo of what Wes is experiencing. Her other hand, almost without thought, presses firmly against her abdomen, a primal, protective gesture as she feels a sudden, restless flutter from within, the unborn child reacting to the sudden, intense surge of energy.

She takes a shaky breath, steadying herself as the raw intensity recedes, leaving behind the lingering awe and the chilling clarity of the concepts Wes just perceived. She hears Sera's concerned voice over the comms, but her focus is locked on Wes.

His final question hangs in the silent, dust-filled air, resonating with the very core of the vision she just partially witnessed. She looks at him, her blue eyes wide with the enormity of what he's suggesting, what the Force itself seems to be implying.

**"Wes..."** she breathes, her voice a hushed whisper over the comm, filled with a mixture of awe, reverence, and a dawning, terrifying sense of destiny. **"I... I felt it. The recognition. The... the *purpose* of this place."** She shakes her head slowly, still trying to process the fragmented echoes. **"The Path awaits the Pathfinder... If the Wayfinder is the key... and this ship is the lock... then..."** She lets the sentence hang, her gaze locked with his, the unspoken answer a tangible, world-altering truth between them. **"...then yes. I think... I think you might be."**

The silence before Marina spoke was deafening.  It felt as if she needed to gather her own thoughts and emotions on the Matter.  His question seemed almost grandiose in asking.  How could he be the pivotal point in all this?   Why him?  Why a young Mandalorian with no training?   It didn’t make sense.   Yet here he was listening to the woman who was carrying his child.  The same woman he had bound himself to and shared an unheard of bond with.   He listened as she came to the same almost unthinkable conclusion.  He saw the answer in her eyes before he heard it.  He also saw the impact it had at the realization.   


He may be the Pathfinder and he was going to need to interact with the Wayfinder again when they got back. 


Disbelief rose within him as the enormous weight that may have just been placed upon his armored shoulders.  He was quiet for a few seconds as he looked around and his sight fell upon the open doors of container 28-C.   They still had a job to do.  He reached out to Marina and let his presence and strength join with her and their baby.   **”Echo stay on overwatch.   Roric, work with Marina and Sera and I on loading the two cargo lifters that we should have control over now.  Maybe we will find something else we will need.”**


He returned his focus back to the ship.  The revelation was still forefront in his mind and the implications were not to be taken lightly.   ***”I’ll come back with the key.   I don’t know when but I will.”***   


He keyed the comms again.  **”Any last questions before I break contact?”**

As Wes projects his final, silent promise to the ship, he feels a subtle, affirmative pulse in return through the Force connection. It's not a reply, but a sense of acknowledgment, of quiescent waiting. The deep, resonant hum in the chamber seems to soften ever so slightly, settling into a state of profound dormancy. The ship has heard him.

Marina gives Wes a final, steadying nod, drawing strength from his renewed focus and the reassuring touch of his presence in the Force. **"No last questions from me, Wes,"** she says over the comm, her voice now regaining its professional calm. **"Let's get this salvage loaded."** She turns her attention to the open maw of Container 28-C, ready to direct Roric and Wes to the specific containment racks holding the hyperdrive coils.

**"I've got the cargo lifters' remote protocols ready to go,"** Sera reports, her own focus shifting back to the practical task at hand. **"Just give me the word, and I'll send the activation command to their charging alcoves on the far side of the bay. They should be trundling over here in a few minutes. And no, no more questions for the ghost in the machine from me. I think we've learned enough for one day."**
**"Understood,"** Roric grunts, his rifle still trained on the corridor. **"Ready to assist with the loading."**
**"Maintaining overwatch,"** Echo's voice confirms, a silent guardian in the shadows above. **"The bay remains clear."**

Within the Force Wes felt the affirmative pulse from the ship.  It felt as if it understood what he had said, an acknowledgement if anything else.   When the others reported back there were no more questions he nodded and slowly removed his hands from the droid.  He felt the withdrawal of the connection and it was unlike anything his limited experience had come in contact with.  


As he stepped back Sera’s voice was coming across the comms.  They still had a mission to do.   As much as he wanted to find out more, their home needed these supplies for its continued operation and existence.   **”Copy Sera.  Get them rolling.  Let’s see if we can locate what we need before they get here.”**  As he walked away from the droid he took one last look at the giant crystal that seemed to be the heart of the ship.  **”Lets get what we need and go home.”**

With Wes's command, the intense, almost spiritual focus of the last few minutes shatters, replaced by the crisp efficiency of a mission in progress. Wes steps back from the now-inert droid, the withdrawal of the ship's profound Force-presence leaving a strange quiet in his senses.

**"Activating the lifters now,"** Sera's voice chirps over the comm. She taps a final sequence into her datapad. Far across the vast, dark expanse of the hangar bay, a series of faint, grinding sounds echo, followed by the low hum of ancient repulsorlift engines whining to life. Two sets of dim, functional lights flicker on in the distance, beginning a slow, methodical journey across the dusty deck towards their position.

Wes, Marina, and Roric, covered by Echo's overwatch, move into the open maw of Container 28-C. Their suit lights cut through the absolute darkness within, revealing a cavernous space. Massive, archaic containment racks, built from the same dark, dense metal as the ship's hull, line the walls. Secured within these racks by heavy magnetic clamps are several colossal, cylindrical objects – the Hyperdrive Energizer Coils. They are caked in a thick, undisturbed layer of dust, but their underlying structure appears remarkably intact.

Marina moves forward, her scanner already active, playing over the nearest coil. **"This is it,"** she confirms, her voice filled with a mixture of awe and professional excitement. **"Mark IV 'Star-Leap' series. The energy readings are... dormant, but the core crystalline matrices appear stable. No signs of critical degradation."** She runs a gloved hand over one of the massive clamps securing it. **"These clamps are magnetically sealed. We'll need to carefully route power from a portable pack to release them without causing a surge. They're massive, Wes. Each one must weigh several tons. We'll definitely need those lifters."**

Just as she finishes speaking, the two Class-4 Cargo Lifter Droids trundle into the light of their position. They are low, flat-bed repulsor sleds, more machine than droid, with multiple articulated lifting arms folded onto their surfaces. They move with a slow, ponderous grace, coming to a halt a respectful distance from the team, their single, glowing photoreceptors awaiting instructions. The path to securing their first primary objective is clear.

Wes nodded when Sera said that the lifters were on their way.  **”Sera I know you’re working on multiple things but I have a question.  The comms that the ship opened, is it just local to this area of the ship or could it be used to possibly communicate with Kael?”** 


As Sera divided her attention on another thing he and Roric followed Marina into the colossal container.  It didn’t take long to find them.  Even with Marina leading them right to their primary mission objective they probably would have found them without the help.   They were massive in size.  Probably the largest things in the containers.  

 
 **”Portable pack.  Will the one we used earlier do?”**  As he spoke some dim illumination from outside appeared from the two load lifters.  **”Good timing.  Alright let’s get the clamps dealt with and our bounty loaded up.”**

Sera's voice comes over the comms, slightly distracted as she multitasks. **"The ship's internal comms... based on the network integrity readings, it's highly localized, Wes. Looks like it's just this engineering sub-level. I doubt the signal could reach all the way up to the hangar bay, especially with a dozen decks of damaged ship in between. We're better off sticking to our encrypted suit comms for team-wide communication."**

Marina looks up from the massive magnetic clamp she's examining, considering Wes's question about the portable power pack. **"The one I used for the turbolift should have enough charge, but these clamps are much larger,"** she replies, her mind clearly working through the technical problem. **"I'll need to be careful to modulate the power output. A sudden, uncontrolled jolt could either fuse the clamp shut or, worse, send a damaging surge into the coil's core matrix."**

She pulls out a small, specialized tool from her kit. **"I can attach a power regulator to the pack. It'll take a minute to calibrate, but it will ensure a clean, stable energy transfer to release the locks safely."**

Without waiting, she kneels before the colossal clamp. The movement, encumbered by the EVA suit and the subtle but undeniable shift in her body's balance, is more deliberate than her usual fluid grace. She takes a moment to adjust her position on the dusty deck, a picture of focused concentration. With practiced movements, she attaches the power regulator to her portable pack, then carefully connects the output leads to an archaic-looking auxiliary power input on the clamp's housing. Her visor is down, its internal display reflecting in her eyes as she monitors the energy flow.

**"Alright, I'm calibrated,"** she reports over the comm, her voice low and steady. **"I'm going to apply a low-level charge to activate the release mechanism. The trick is to give it just enough juice to disengage the magnetic field without overloading the ancient circuits."**

Her gloved fingers gently adjust a dial on the regulator. A low hum emanates from the clamp, and the air around it seems to shimmer for a moment. The massive metal arms holding the hyperdrive coil in place remain stubbornly locked. She makes another minute adjustment, her breath held. For a second, nothing happens. Then, with a deep, resonant **THUNK-HISS**, the magnetic field collapses, and the massive metallic arms retract smoothly into their housings, leaving the colossal hyperdrive coil resting free in its cradle for the first time in millennia.

**"First one is free,"** Marina announces, a clear note of satisfaction in her voice as she detaches her equipment. Pushing herself up from her kneeling position is a two-part motion, her hand bracing against her thigh for leverage. She lets out a soft, almost inaudible sigh of exertion as she rises and her hand goes to her lower back for a moment from the strain. **"It's stable. Roric, Wes, let's get one of the lifters into position for this coil while I move on to the next clamp."** She begins to move towards the second set of locks, her steps measured and careful in the bulky suit.

The loud **THUNK-HISS** meant they were in business.  Looking at Marina, Wes saw her hunched down on the dusty deck, working at releasing the other clamp.  He knew she would never hint at it to anyone, even him but he could feel that this was extra effort especially now.  


He loved her and their growing baby inside her.   


He let those feelings extend out to encompass her and smiled when she looked up, even if only for a second.  Her smile in return before turning back to her task at hand only made the feelings stronger.   **”Alright Ror let’s get a lifter up in here and get this one out.”**  He said as he saw her stand and with some effort.  


As Marina moved to the next one Wes walked out to the opening of the massive container where the two lifters waited hovering a meter above the dusty deck plating.  **”Alright you,”**  He pointed at one on the left.  **”Let's go, I'll point it out.”**  He wasn’t sure if the droid lifters would respond to voice commands but it was worth as shot.

Wes steps out from the container into the vast, silent hangar. The two Class-4 Cargo Lifter Droids hover patiently, their single photoreceptors glowing with a dim, passive light. As Wes points and issues his verbal command, the targeted droid remains perfectly still, its ancient systems awaiting a digital signal, not a spoken one.

Before the silence can become awkward, Sera's voice, laced with amusement, crackles over the comm channel. **"A little less conversation, a little more remote procedure call, Wes. I've got it."** Her fingers tap a quick sequence on the datapad she's still holding. **"Alright, Lifter Unit 02, you heard the man. Designating target coordinates inside Container 28-C. Proceed with caution."**

A series of soft clicks and whirs emanates from the targeted lifter droid. Its repulsorlift engines whine to a slightly higher pitch, and it pivots slowly, its movements ponderous but smooth. It begins to glide forward, following an invisible path towards the open container door.

**"Copy that,"** Roric grunts, stepping forward from his overwatch position. **"Moving to guide it in."** He walks alongside the slowly advancing lifter, his armored hand gesturing to ensure it clears the container's opening, ready to help maneuver it into the tight space.

Inside the container, Marina, having just reached the next set of magnetic clamps, pauses her work to watch the approach of the heavy-duty repulsor sled. The process of securing their first, critical piece of salvage is now underway.

Wes stood back out of the way as the lifter slowly advanced into position.   They were working together like a well oiled machine.   **”Nice work team.  Let's get these out and on their way back to Kael.”**  


They were going to need a way to let Kael know what was going on.   **”Sera I know the ship's comms won’t work between here and the hangar bay but could we rig something to let Kael know what is happening?  Can the lifters broadcast a short prerecorded message when they reach the hangar?”**

Sera looks up from her datapad, where she's monitoring the lifter's slow progress into the container. A thoughtful, impressed expression crosses her face as she considers Wes's idea.

**"That's... actually a really clever workaround, Wes,"** she admits, a genuine smile in her voice. **"These lifters are ancient; they definitely don't have a long-range comms transmitter built in. But,"** she pulls up a subsystem diagnostic on her screen, **"they *do* have basic proximity alert emitters. Simple audio speakers designed to beep or play a warning chime when they're moving."**

She taps a few commands, her eyes scanning the resulting code. **"Their command buffer is tiny, but... yes. I can piggyback a small, compressed audio file onto its primary return-path command. I'll set a geofence trigger. The moment its internal sensors confirm it's within five meters of the *Skimmer's* IFF signature, it will play the file through its external speaker."**

She looks up at him. **"It won't be encrypted, so anyone in the hangar will hear it, but it'll get the message to Kael. Just record what you want to say. Keep it short – under ten seconds, to be safe. I'll package it and upload it to Lifter 02's queue."**

He nodded at Sera’s suggestion of a short message to be played when the lifters reached Kael in the hangar bay.   **”On it.”**  With a top on his bracer he activated the record function.  ***”Main objective complete.  Moving to others.  Will send other objectives when able.  Wes out.”***  He spoke quickly and clearly and the message was short enough like Sera requested.    With another tap he sent the message along to the Zeltron.  


With the message to Kael taken care of, he moved closer to Marina.  **”Anything else in this container that may prove useful?  Nothing that will take too long though.  We still need to get to the other two locations.”**

Marina looks up from her position on the deck, the soft light from her visor illuminating Wes's armored form standing over her. She gives a grateful nod as the second massive **THUNK-HISS** signals the release of the final clamp on the second coil.

**"Okay, that's the second coila completely free,"** she confirms, her voice a little strained from the focused effort. There was only one coil left. She doesn't immediately move to stand, instead taking a moment to catch her breath and shift her weight in the cumbersome suit.

She hears his question about other useful salvage in the container. Tapping the side of her helmet to bring her datapad's display into her visor's field of view, she quickly scans the manifest data she and Sera had unlocked, filtering it for items specifically logged to Container 28-C.

**"Let me check the manifest for this specific container..."** she murmurs, her eyes flicking back and forth as she reads. **"Okay... besides the coils, it lists a 'Series-7 Diagnostic Array' for this type of energizer. It's probably obsolete, but Gaxx might be able to cannibalize it. It looks bulky, though... probably a low-priority grab given our time."**

Her gaze shifts, and she points with a gloved hand towards a secured rack deeper inside the container. **"But... it *also* lists several bundles of shielded, high-capacity power conduits. Those are definitely on Gaxx's secondary 'wish list'. They look much easier to secure quickly than the diagnostic rig. We could probably have Roric grab them while we load the main coil."** She looks back up at Wes, presenting the options, leaving the final decision to him.

Wes watched as Marina worked from the position she had taken.  He could hear the slight bit of strain in her voice.   Her movements to most would seem normal but he also knew her on a different level then the others.   He could not only see with his eyes but feel within the Force the effort she was putting forth.   His question on anything else being useful within the current container has two possibilities.   The second option being the better one given their time constraints.  


He nodded as he held out his hand to her.  He knew she was capable but even he knew that getting over extended was just as bad.  **”High-capacity power conduits it is then.   If by some means we can grab the diagnostic array we will but it’s not a priority.”**   He looked over at Roric as he helped pull Marina to her feet.  **”Roric, Marina is going to send the location of some power conduits.   Go and secure what you can.   She and I will handle loading this second coil.”**

**"Copy that, Wes,"** Roric's voice crackles in reply. He gives a curt nod and moves deeper into the container's cavernous interior, his suit lights sweeping towards the racks Marina had indicated. **"On the conduits."**

Marina accepts Wes's offered hand, the strength of his grip a welcome assistance as he helps her to her feet. The simple, supportive gesture speaks volumes. **"Thanks, Wes,"** she murmurs, her voice a private aside over their comms. **"Alright, sending the conduit location to Roric's HUD now."** She then looks at the second lifter droid hovering patiently just outside the container entrance. **"Sera, can you guide Unit 04 in? We can get both coils loaded in parallel."**

**"Already on it,"** Sera confirms from her position near the container's entrance. **"Bringing Lifter 04 into the container now. It'll be a tight fit with both of them in here, so watch your feet."**

As Sera directs it via her datapad, the second Cargo Lifter Droid glides smoothly into the massive container, its repulsorlifts humming quietly. It maneuvers carefully around the first lifter, which Wes and Marina are now guiding into position around the first freed coil. Roric, meanwhile, has reached the secured bundles of thick, shielded power conduits and begins working on their release clamps. The team operates with a newfound efficiency, a small pocket of purposeful activity in the vast, silent tomb of the *Veiled Shadow*.

Wes smiles at Sera’s joke about watching their feet.   **”Copy that Sera.  Keeping the feet out of the way.  Let’s see how good you drive that thing.”**  He said wit( a smile as he looked out the container's doors as the lifter maneuvered its way in under Sera’s skilled control.  


**”Echo, status report.   How are we looking out there?”**  As he waited for the update he looked at Marina.  From the light their suits showed they could see each other's faces.  He switched to their private channel.  **”Good work Rin.”**  He gave her a smile as he used the name he first knew her by.  It was almost a private thing between them.

Echo's voice remains a calm, steady presence over the comms, a counterpoint to the team's focused activity within the container. **"All quiet out here, Wes. No change in status. The bay remains silent. No movement, no new energy readings on my scanners. Just dust and ghosts. Overwatch continues."**

Marina hears Wes's quiet praise over their private channel. Even through the tinted visor of his helmet, she can sense the warmth and pride in his words, a feeling that flows through their bond and brings a genuine, tired smile to her own face. **"We make a good team,"** she transmits back, her voice soft. **"Now let's get this beast secured."** She turns her full attention to the lifter droid, gesturing with her hand to guide its articulated arms, ensuring they lock onto the massive hyperdrive coil with a firm, balanced grip. The ancient, heavy component settles onto the repulsor sled with a deep, metallic groan.

Working in tandem, Wes and Marina guide the first lifter droid's articulated arms. With a series of satisfying **CLUNKS**, the heavy manipulators lock securely around the colossal hyperdrive coil, securing it to the repulsor sled's magnetic clamps.

On the other side of the bay, Roric finishes unclamping a heavy bundle of the shielded power conduits. He hefts the coiled mass onto his shoulder with a grunt.
**"First bundle of conduits is free,"** he reports, his voice slightly strained from the effort. **"Ready for transport."**

**"Copy that, Roric,"** Marina replies, already moving to the second coil where Sera has expertly maneuvered the other lifter into position. **"You can load that onto Lifter 02 with the first coil. I'll secure it from here so you can go back for the rest."**

Roric lumbers over and carefully places the heavy bundle of conduits onto the first cargo lifter, near the base of the secured hyperdrive coil. Marina, using a small control panel on the lifter itself, activates a series of smaller magnetic restraints that clamp down on the conduits, locking them in place for the journey back to the *Void Skimmer*.

With the first bundle secured, Roric gives a curt nod and heads back towards the storage racks to retrieve the remaining conduits. Meanwhile, Wes joins Marina at the second coil, and together they guide the arms of Lifter 04, securing their second primary objective onto its repulsor sled. The process is smooth, efficient, a testament to their growing teamwork.

Working alongside one another the two lovers fell into a groove.  Each knowing or having a feeling of what was coming next.  It could have been the deeper closeness or the Force that afforded the effectiveness of their work.   Perhaps it was a combination of the two.   **”There we go.   Easy does it.”**. Wes said more to himself as helped guide the coil to its place on Lifter 04.


He began to secure the massive coil as he looked to where the last one was waiting.   **“One more to grab and then on to the next.  Either one will be time consuming considering how far they are and the compromised hull for one of them.”**

Marina watches as Wes secures the second hyperdrive coil onto Lifter 04. She then glances over at the third, final coil still resting in its ancient cradle, a silent, multi-ton problem.

**"Wes,"** she says, her voice coming over the comm, practical and focused. **"We only have two functional lifters. Lifter 03 is offline, remember? We can only take two coils on this trip."** She looks from the third coil back to the two loaded sleds. **"One of these lifters will have to come all the way back from the *Skimmer* to get the last one. That's going to add significant time to the op if we decide it's worth it. We should probably get these first two en route back to Kael now."**

Wes nodded at Marina.   She was right on the time.    While it would be good to procure a spare coil now wasn’t the time.  **”Let's get these on their way and figure out our next move.”**. He motioned for them to head to the mouth of the container where Sera was.   


He came to a stop as Sera was tapping away on her datapad.   **”Sera, let's get these on their way to Kael.   What’s the possibility of using them with the next two objectives?   I know one will be tough to get to but if we can make it a bit easier it’s worth trying.”**

Sera looks up from her datapad as Wes and Marina approach the mouth of the container. The two loaded lifter droids hover silently behind them, their single photoreceptors glowing.

**"Alright, Wes,"** she confirms, her fingers flying across the screen. **"Programming the return route for Lifters 02 and 04 now. Destination: Hangar Bay, rendezvous coordinates at the *Void Skimmer's* ramp. They'll follow the path we took, so it'll be a slow, steady trip back."** She taps a final command. **"They're on their way. I've set them to broadcast your pre-recorded message to Kael on proximity."**

With a low hum, the two massive repulsor sleds pivot smoothly and begin their ponderous journey out of the container and back down the long, dark corridor towards the distant hangar bay, their dim lights slowly receding into the gloom.

Sera then considers his question about using the lifters for the other objectives. She shakes her head. **"Negative on that, I'm afraid. Their operational range is limited to this cargo deck. The schematics show they're slaved to this section's local power grid and pathfinding system. They can't access the turbolifts or navigate the ship's other primary sectors. We're on foot to get to the bridge and main engineering."**

Wes watched the two repulsor sleds that carried their precious cargo begin their journey back to the hangar bay into Kael’s capable hands.  Their primary objective was complete on their end.  Now it was on to the others.  With Sera confirming what he thought might be the case with the lifters he nodded.   


**”Undersood.  We’ll figure it out.  Good work team.”**  He brought up his left arm and tapped the screen of his bracer computer.   **”Next objective.  Navigational Matrix Stabilizers.  Let’s map out the quickest and or the most efficient way there.”**

With the primary objective secured and on its way back to the *Void Skimmer*, a renewed sense of purpose energizes the team. The immediate danger of the crystal has been replaced by the focused, methodical process of a salvage operation.

**"Alright, Wes,"** Marina's voice comes over the comms, a holographic schematic of the ship's upper decks appearing on their HUDs. **"I've highlighted the most direct route to the bridge on Deck 2. It involves taking this turbolift up, then traversing several primary conduits. Should be straightforward, but it's a long walk."**

Sera, however, lets out a low whistle as she cross-references Marina's route with the full system data she'd accessed. **"Straightforward, maybe, but look at this,"** she says, adding a new overlay to their HUDs. A series of bright red icons – the dormant "Hazard Containment" droids – populates the main corridor Marina had indicated. **"That path takes us right through at least two, maybe three, patrols of those things. Even dormant, I'd rather not play hopscotch with them if we can avoid it."**

She zooms in on the schematic, tracing a different path. **"The alternate maintenance corridor the ship showed us is an option, but it's a tight, winding route. It would be slow."** She pauses, her finger tracing a massive power conduit on the schematic that runs parallel to their current deck. **"Here's something weird, though. Look at this power conduit. It runs the length of this deck and according to the grid, it's supposed to terminate at that bulkhead... but my diagnostic shows a faint, persistent power drain coming from a section of dead space right behind it. There's an unlisted void on the schematics, big enough to be a secondary hangar, but it's not on the map."**

As Sera describes the anomaly, Wes focuses on the location she indicated on his own HUD. He reaches out with the Force, and a jolt of recognition, a distinct feeling of wrongness, hits him. It's not the deep, ancient resonance of the crystal. This is a discordant note, an echo of technology that feels millennia younger than the rest of the ship, a pocket of something familiar yet utterly out of place, caged within the ancient vessel.

Wes looked at both options they had on getting to the bridge.  The first option from Marina was straight forward but it meant that they had to cross the path of two, possibly three of the colossal maintenance droids.   The same droids that at some point may have “cleaned the crew”.  


The other option was Sera’s.  The red skinned Zeltron laid out another path.  One using the alternate maintenance corridor.  It was a more roundabout route that would take more time at the cost of avoiding the droids.  As Sera noticed an anomaly on the schematic he brought it up on his HUD.  The power conduit was supposed to stop at the bulkhead but she was right.   There was a power drain coming from the significantly large dead space behind it.  **”Thats a very large “dead” space and we all know that space is at a premium for a ship no matter the size.”**


Turning slightly he looked at the bulkhead and reached out with the Force.  As he pushed out past the wall something immediately hit him.    A recognition of something familiar and a sense that something was wrong.   It felt completely out of place on this ancient vessel.  A piece or pieces of technology that didn’t belong in the time this ship was in its heyday.  **”There’s something in there.   Something that’s out of place that doesn’t belong”**  He saw Sera tilt her head as he spoke.  Clearly wondering what it was he sensed.  **”I don’t know how to describe it other than it feels younger than this vessel.  Something mechanical possibly.”**  


He looked back at the bulkhead and there was something important beyond it.  **”Do we have a way in there?”**  The tone in his voice was one of certainty that they needed to check it out.

The holographic schematic floats between Wes and Sear showing the routes to their next objectives, with Sera's newly discovered anomaly highlighted. Sera studies the "dead space" on the schematic, her professional curiosity piqued by Wes's Force-sense confirmation. **"A way in? Not according to this map,"** she says, tapping the blank area on the holo-display. **"As far as the *Veiled Shadow's* original plans are concerned, this is just a solid block of superstructure. But if there's a power drain and you're sensing something... that means it was added later. An unauthorized, unlisted modification."** She zooms in on the power conduit she'd identified. **"If there's an entrance, it's either hidden or was sealed up after the fact. The most likely access would be from the service corridor that runs parallel to this bulkhead, but there's no door marked on the plans."**

Marina considers the tactical and technical implications. **"If it's an unlisted modification, it could be anything,"** she adds, her voice cautious. **"A hidden smuggling compartment, a secret lab, or... another cargo bay that was sealed off for some reason. If we want to check it out, we'll have to investigate that service corridor physically. It's a detour from our direct route to the bridge, but it avoids at least one of those dormant droid patrols."**
**"A detour into an unknown, unmapped section?"** Roric's gruff voice cuts in over the comm. **"Sounds like a good way to get lost or walk into a trap. Our objective is the bridge."**

Echo, however, offers a different perspective. **"But if there's something 'younger' in there, as Wes says,"** she reasons, her tone calm and analytical, **"it might be from a more recognizable technological era. That could mean salvageable tech that's actually compatible with our own systems. And an unlisted bay is a place no one would think to look for supplies. It's a risk, but it could be a significant opportunity."**

Wes listened as they all had a voice on this just discovered ‘secret compartment’.   They all raised good points.  Roric especially.   **”Roric you raise a good point.  The next objective is the bridge.  The rest of you raise good points as well.  We won't devote any unnecessary time to this.   We take the service corridor that allows us to bypass at least one of the droid checkpoints.   If,”**  He paused as he emphasized the last word.  **”If we do manage to find a way in, we take a quick look.”**  He looked at Sera and then to Marina.  **”With the size and expanse of that unknown space could there be a possibility of another way to the bridge?  Does that sound like it could even add up?”**

Marina considers Wes's question, cross-referencing the partial schematics on her HUD with the implications of Sera's discovery. **"A direct route from a hidden bay to the bridge... it's unlikely in any standard ship design, Wes. Bridges are usually isolated, hardened targets for security reasons. You wouldn't want a smuggler's entrance leading right to the command center."**

She pauses, her gaze turning towards the wall where the anomaly is located. **"However, this is anything but a standard ship. If it was a private captain's launch or a VIP shuttle bay, it's *possible* it could have its own secure, direct-access turbolift that bypasses the main corridors. We won't know for sure unless we find an entrance and see what's inside."**

Sera nods in agreement. **"And if we find an entrance, there might be a local control terminal inside,"** she adds, her voice keen with a slicer's optimism. **"If it's an isolated system, it might have its own set of schematics that aren't on the main network we just cracked. That would be our best bet for finding a true layout of this place, or a back door to the bridge."**

With the new plan set, the team pivots. They leave the massive cargo conduit behind, their suit lights now probing the entrance to a narrower, more utilitarian service corridor that runs parallel to it. The scale here is more human, but the darkness feels deeper, the silence more claustrophobic. Echo melts into the lead, her movements economical and silent, as she begins the methodical process of clearing the path ahead, one shadowed corner at a time.

The service corridor is a stark contrast to the grand, echoing passages they've traversed so far. It's a tight, almost claustrophobic tunnel of dark metal, ribbed with thick conduits and power lines that snake along the walls and ceiling. Dust hangs heavy in the airless space, and their suit lights reveal centuries of neglect in the form of corroded panels and dangling, severed cables. The team moves in a tight, single-file formation, the silence broken only by the quiet static of their comms and the sound of their own breathing.

**"Corridor's clear for another thirty meters,"** Echo's voice whispers over the channel, her beam a steady presence probing the darkness ahead. **"Multiple maintenance hatches, all sealed. No power readings."**

They proceed for what feels like a long, tense ten minutes, the passage twisting and turning. The only landmark is the massive, featureless bulkhead to their left – the wall that, according to Sera's scans, conceals the unlisted "dead space."
Suddenly, Echo freezes, raising a gloved hand in a silent halt signal. **"Hold,"** she transmits, her voice low. **"I've got something."**

The team stacks up behind her, their weapons at a low ready. Echo's light is fixed on a section of the bulkhead. To the untrained eye, it looks identical to the rest of the corridor wall. But to a scout's practiced gaze, something is off.

**"The dust pattern,"** she reports, her voice a hushed whisper. **"It's different here. Thinner. And look at the floor."** She points her light down, illuminating a series of faint, almost invisible scratches on the deck plating, partially obscured by the thick dust – marks that suggest something very large and very heavy was dragged or moved here, and not in the distant, ancient past, but more recently. Recently, in the context of a ship that has been dead for millennia, could still mean centuries ago, but it's a significant disruption in the otherwise pristine decay.

Sera moves up, her own light scanning the wall where Echo indicated. **"She's right,"** she confirms. **"And... my proximity scanner is picking up a faint trace of a different metallic alloy behind this panel. It's not the same composite as the rest of the ship's hull."**

Marina joins them, running her own diagnostic scanner over the area. **"I'm getting it too. And there's a hairline seam. Almost invisible. This isn't just a wall panel, Wes. It's a door. A very well-hidden one."**
There are no visible controls, no handle, no keypad. Just a massive, seamless section of the corridor wall that is, apparently, not a wall at all.

With the decision made they headed out for the bridge.  Their current path.   The corridor that ran parallel to the unknown yet massive void on the ship’s schematics.   While they didn’t really have time to go off mission he had made it clear that they wouldn’t push to find entry unless things fell into place.   Luck, weather for good or bad. Seemed to grace them once again.  That luck was that someone else had been here before.   Decades if not centuries before they stepped aboard on their mission of need.     


He looked at the dust patterns that Echo pointed out.   That something heavy had been moved or dragged through the ‘door’ that was very well hidden.  If they hadn't been looking for something out of place they might have missed it.    He stepped closer to the wall and looked at the hairline seam that Marina had pointed at.   


Running his fingers up and down the seam there was nothing that gave way and no obvious controls.  **”Different metal composition behind the wall.  Hairline seam with no controls.”**  He looked at Marina.  He smiled beneath his helmet.  She had taught him to look at things differently and not always used the hammer approach.  **”Perhaps there is a mechanism behind the wall.”**


With the fingers of his left hand on the seam he reached out and projected his presence past the wall.  In search for a way to open the massive door

As Wes places his hand on the cool metal and extends his senses, he pushes past the ancient, dormant presence of the *Veiled Shadow's* hull. The contrast is immediate and jarring. Behind the wall, he feels something distinctly *younger*, its Force signature, while inert, feeling like a product of the last century, not the last millennia. It's a complex, unpowered electronic locking mechanism, linked to a heavy physical bolt system. His senses pinpoint a specific spot, a recessed access panel hidden beneath a seamless-looking plate, where the control interface must be. The feeling is certain: this door is an addition, a modification made long after the ship was built, a secret hidden within a secret.
Marina, observing Wes's intense focus, moves closer. **"You found something?"** she asks over the comms, her voice a low murmur.
Sera also steps forward, her scanner now aimed at the spot Wes is focused on. **"That different alloy I detected... it's concentrated right there. There's something behind that plate."**

**"Let me see,"** Marina says, moving to the spot Wes has identified. She pulls a thin magnetic resonator from her kit and runs it over the seamless wall. The device emits a series of soft clicks, mapping the density behind the plate. **"There. The locking pins for the access cover are here... here... and here."**

With a surgeon's precision, she uses a fine-tipped sonic pick, touching it to the three points she identified. A low, almost inaudible hum vibrates through the metal. With a final touch, there's a soft *thump*, and a rectangular section of the wall pops open a fraction of a centimeter, revealing a recessed panel with an electronic lock and an auxiliary power port, just as Wes had sensed.

**"Panel's exposed,"** she confirms. **"The lock is electronic, but completely unpowered. I can try to hotwire it with my portable pack, but it's not a quick job. I'll have to isolate the right circuits and feed it a stable current without frying the whole mechanism."** She looks up from her work, her helmeted gaze finding Wes's. **"This will take time, Wes. Are you sure you want to commit to this? Your call."**

Wes stepped back as Marina went to work on the nodded access plate that he had found.  In a quick moment he had revealed the hidden access panel that he sensed with the Force.   He shook his head as Marina explained what it would take to bypass the lock.   It was not powered like many of the things they had found.  Which meant they had to take the long road to get it working.  


She looked up at him from where she worked.  Her words rang true.   This was going to take time.  Time they didn’t have.   They had been lucky up till now and maybe even a bit ahead of schedule but this was a major unknown.  He looked at the panel and the area they were in.   Someone within the last century had been where they were standing.  The questions rebounded inside his head.  Who, why and when were they here.    And what were they after?


Through their bond he knew she could feel him wrestling with the decision.  The seconds dragged on and she finally felt him come to a decision.  He shook his head.  Echo’s previous comment about new tech more aligned with their time tugged at him but this was seriously off mission parameters.  Then there was the feeling within the Force.  Teachings from master Rukh to trust in the Force came forth.   **”Open it.  Ten minutes.  If it can’t be opened at that time we move to the bridge and mark this location for a return trip.”**

The ten minutes begin to tick by in the oppressive silence of the service corridor. Marina works with a surgeon's precision, attaching delicate probes and a power modulator to the hidden panel. Her visor is down, data scrolling across its internal display as she carefully isolates the circuits needed to power the lock without frying it.

Beside her, Sera's fingers are a blur across her own datapad, which is slaved to Marina's probes. **"I'm reading the lock's architecture now,"** she whispers over the comms. **"It's a custom job. High-end corporate security, maybe fifty or sixty years old. Definitely not from the same era as the rest of this rust bucket. It's got multiple counter-intrusion loops, but they're all dormant without main power. If you can give me a clean, stable energy feed, I can bypass them sequentially."**
Minutes feel like hours. Wes watches his chrono, the numbers ticking down: seven minutes... five... three... Echo and Roric remain perfectly still at their posts, silent sentinels.

**"Okay, Sera... I've got a stable power loop isolated,"** Marina announces, her voice tight with concentration. **"It's not much, but it should be enough to energize the lock's primary logic board. Sending power... now."**
A series of soft clicks emanate from the hidden panel as the ancient electronics receive power for the first time in decades, maybe centuries.
**"I see it. Firewalls are coming online... weak,"** Sera reports. **"Bypassing first loop... second loop... got it. Unlocking primary magnetic bolts... now!"**

With a deep, satisfying **CHUNK**, followed by the smooth, almost silent hiss of modern pneumatics, the massive, hidden door glides sideways into the bulkhead, revealing a dark, cavernous space beyond. The sound is shockingly quiet compared to the groaning protest of the ship's original doors.

The team's suit lights cut into the new darkness, revealing a secondary, smaller hangar bay. The air here is also vacuum, the deck coated in a thinner, but still significant, layer of dust. In the center of the bay, its landing struts settled deep in the ancient grime, sits a ship. Unlike the ancient, alien design of the *Veiled Shadow*, this vessel is instantly recognizable, though clearly old and in a state of disrepair: a Ghtroc 720 light freighter. It is dark, silent, and appears to have been here for a very long time.
**"By the Force..."** Marina breathes, her light playing over the familiar, rugged lines of the Ghtroc.
**"Well,"** Sera says, a note of pure professional surprise in her voice. **"That is definitely not on any manifest."**

Wes watched them work all the while keeping an eye on the timer he had started on his HUD.   He looked to where Echo and Roric had taken up positions.  Their ever vigilant guardians were on the lookout as the door controls were worked on by a talented team.    


They were past the three minute mark and it was looking like that they might not make the time table he had set.   Finally at around the minute thirty point Marina declared she had a stable power routed to the door controls.  Sera was quick to jump in with the slicing.   What seemed like long seconds finally ended as he heard the Zeltron say ‘now’.  


With the telltale sound of a locking mechanism letting go he watched as the massive hidden door moved in relative silence to the ancient ship's old mechanics.   The darkness was sliced up but their suit lights revealed something that none of them expected.  Something that was as out of place as they were on the ancient ship.   Before them was an older Ghtroc 720 light freighter.  He smiled at the freighter.  Thankful that the Force didn’t lead him down a path of follies.   


As Marina and Sera both expressed their surprise he found himself nodding.  **”I agree with both of you.   I would have believed it if I was told ahead of time.”**  He surveyed the area slightly with the lights.  **”Alright it’s open, let's check it out.  Thirty minute time frame then we reevaluate the situation to see if it warrants continuing.  Echo, Roric let’s clear a path.  By the numbers let’s make sure nothing was left in surprise for any interlopers.”**  


He stepped back to let the two through.   **”Good work you two.   Finding out any info about that ship, cargo, why it’s here and for what purpose is next on the list.   Schematics of the bay and anything else you can find.”**

**"Copy, Wes. Thirty-minute clock. Moving to clear,"** Echo's voice confirms. She advances into the dusty, silent hangar bay, her carbine's light methodically sweeping the corners, the underside of the Ghtroc, and the surrounding walls. Her movements are a textbook example of a tactical sweep, fluid and alert.

Roric follows a few steps behind, his heavier rifle covering the angles Echo isn't, providing overlapping fields of fire. **"On your six, Echo,"** he grunts, his gaze scanning the higher gantries and shadowy alcoves of the hidden bay.

**"Acknowledged,"** Marina replies, her gaze already fixed on the Ghtroc. **"I'll start with a full external scan, check for hull integrity, any external power sources, or signs of what might have disabled it. If I can find a maintenance or cargo port, I'll see if I can interface with its systems without powering it up."**

**"I'll work the local network,"** Sera adds, already moving toward a dusty terminal near the hangar's main control station. **"If this bay was a later addition, it might have its own logs or control systems separate from the main ship. I'll search for docking records, cargo manifests, anything that explains what a Ghtroc is doing here. And I'll keep an eye out for any automated defenses specific to this bay."**

The team once again splits into their specialized roles with practiced efficiency. Echo and Roric begin their methodical security sweep of the hangar, their lights cutting precise patterns in the darkness. Marina starts a slow, careful circuit around the ancient Ghtroc, her visor's scanner glowing as she gathers data, while Sera sets up at the dusty control terminal, attempting to breathe life into another long-dead system.

The thirty-minute timer on Wes’s HUD begins its silent countdown. The hidden hangar bay is a hive of quiet, focused activity, the only sounds the faint crackle of comms and the soft hum of their suit systems.

After a few minutes of sweeping the perimeter, Echo's voice comes over the comms, as calm as ever. **"Initial sweep complete. The bay is secure. No energy readings, no signs of recent activity besides our own. Just this ship, a lot of dust, and the silence."** She takes up a watchful position near the Ghtroc's main boarding ramp.
**"Rear is clear,"** Roric confirms, having secured the entrance from the service corridor. **"We're sealed in here for now."**

Marina completes her external circuit of the Ghtroc, her scanner's light playing over its pitted and dust-caked hull. **"Wes, the external hull looks... surprisingly intact,"** she reports, a note of professional surprise in her voice. **"Micrometeoroid pitting is minimal, which means this bay has been sealed for a very long time. I'm not detecting any major structural breaches. However, the power conduits are completely dead, and the main drive looks like it's been cold for decades, maybe longer. The main boarding ramp is sealed tight, but there's an auxiliary cargo hatch on the starboard side that looks promising. The manual release seems to be intact, just seized up with age."**

From her position at the dusty hangar control terminal, Sera lets out a low whistle. **"Okay, this is interesting,"** she transmits. **"I managed to get a trickle of power to this terminal from my portable pack. The local systems here are... a patchwork. It looks like whoever set this bay up grafted a more modern (relatively speaking) system onto the *Veiled Shadow's* ancient infrastructure."**

She continues, her voice quickening with discovery. **"I've accessed a fragmented hangar log. The last entry is dated... almost 75 years ago. It lists this ship, the Ghtroc 720, under the registry 'Seventh Moon'. The log entry is simple: 'Vessel docked for long-term survey preparation. Awaiting final supply manifest before departure.' And that's it. Nothing else. It's like the crew just... vanished before they could leave."**
