**"I'm almost clear!"** Marina shouts over the comm, seeing her opportunity. She pushes the sluggish repulsor sled's controls to their maximum, guiding the massive, hovering power converters past the now-crippled GD-2 and towards the relative safety of the waiting lift.

Despite the solid hit from Ror’s ion rifle and a deep almost severing slash to one of its primary legs from his own lightsaber the droid was still upright and a threat.  Now its full attention was fixated on the fast moving threat.   Him.  


The area was a chaotic mess.   The one cleaner droid that had its optics blown out by Roric was still flailing wildly as Marina and the sled moved farther away from the action.  Neither droid was fairing well.  One was blinded possibly permanently the other was severely disabled.  **”Hit it again Ror.”**   He said calmly as he righted himself, his jetpack leveling him out.   He waited for Ror to take his shot as he gathered his focus as he prepared himself for what he had planned.   As Roric fired he gripped the droid with the Force.   His hand outstretched in focus.  As soon as Ror’s shot hit or missed he dug deep within himself and made a motion with his outstretched hand towards the other blind and flailing cleaner droid.

Roric needs no further encouragement. The veteran soldier already has the crippled, sparking GD-2 in his sights. He adjusts his aim slightly, compensating for the droid's lurching movements. Another brilliant blue ion bolt erupts from his rifle with a sharp **CRACK-HISS**, striking the droid squarely in its central chassis.

GD-2 convulses violently. Its remaining limbs lock up, its damaged leg gives out completely, and it pitches forward, its systems sputtering and shorting out from the massive ion overload. It is completely immobilized, sparks erupting from its joints, though its red eye still flickers with malevolent, powerless intent.

Wes, seeing Roric's shot has completely disabled GD-2, gathers his focus. He levels an outstretched, armored hand at the immobilized droid. The Mandalorian grips the sparking droid with the raw power of the Force.

The air in the antechamber crackles with unseen energy. GD-2, weighing several tons, is torn from the floor with a screech of protesting metal. Servos strain and break as Wes, with a deep, guttural yell of exertion audible over the comms, telekinetically hurls the disabled droid through the air.

The makeshift projectile soars across the chamber and slams with catastrophic force into the blind, flailing GD-1. The impact is a deafening explosion of rending metal and shattering components. The two ancient droids collapse into a single, tangled heap of sparking, ruined machinery. A leg from one is torn free, skittering across the deck plating. The red eye of GD-2 finally goes dark. A profound silence falls over the antechamber, broken only by the faint crackle of dying electronics.

Marina, who had been bracing for another attack, stares in wide-eyed awe at the display of raw power. Through their bond, she felt the immense, focused effort Wes poured into the final, devastating attack. Shaking herself back to the mission, she gives the repulsor sled a final, powerful push, guiding it and its precious cargo safely out of the antechamber and into the entrance of the narrow maintenance conduit. **"Wes... clear. We're clear,"** she transmits, her voice a mixture of relief and astonishment.

Wes watched as Roric’s shot hit the droid dead on.  He watched as the blue energy discharge crackled around the droid.  Roric’s aim was true.  The second ion blade coupled with his earlier attack was enough to overload the droid and shut it down.  


It made his plan easier as he brought his complete focus down on the droid that now crackled with blue energy from Roric’s ion strike.  


His outstretched hand closed into a fist as he gripped it with the Force.  He felt the metal buckle slightly within his telekinetic grip.  It was like training back with Master Rukh.  Now though it was a matter of life and death for him and his companions.   With the roar of his jet packs thrusters keeping him aloft he made a motion with his hand and arm and he sent it at the other cleaner droid.  A loud grunt of exertion followed as the droid was torn from the floor and sent careening across the chamber where it impacted with the second droid.   The resulting crash was deafening as both droids skidded along the decking in a tangled heap.   Neither moved again.


Wes took several deep breaths of air as the roar of his jet pack still sounded.  Marina’s voice over comms that they were clear timed perfectly as he took a deep breath from the exertion of throwing the droid.   **”Copy. Roric and I are following.  The droids are down.  We’ll catch up in a moment.”**  He calmly said over comms through deep breaths as he angled his flight path back to Roric and gently settled down next to the veteran soldier.  He gave him a nod, his T visor moving up and down.  **”Good shooting Roric.”**    He said with a smile beneath his helmet as he closed down his lightsaber.  The green blade quickly disappeared.  He kept the hilt in hand for the moment.   **”Let's catch a ride with Marina back to the ship and let’s see if we can raise the others.”**

Roric lowers his ion rifle, a wisp of smoke curling from its barrel. He had watched Wes's display of raw telekinetic power with the stoic, unreadable expression of a professional soldier witnessing something far outside his normal experience. He simply gives Wes a curt, single nod in acknowledgment of the praise. **"You made it an easy shot, Del-Fin,"** he grunts, his own form of a compliment. **"Let's get that salvage moving."**

Wes and Roric make their way to the entrance of the maintenance conduit, where Marina is waiting with the massive, hovering repulsor sled. The two colossal power converters are secured, a testament to their successful, if chaotic, salvage operation.

The journey back through the cramped, twisting maintenance tunnels is slow and tense. Wes and Roric take up positions at the front and rear of the sled, their weapons ready, while Marina expertly pilots the cumbersome vehicle through the narrow passages. The powerful Force resonance from the reactor chamber slowly fades behind them, replaced by the simple, quiet hum of the sled's repulsorlifts.

After what feels like an eternity, they emerge from the maintenance hatch back into the familiar, if structurally compromised, main engineering corridor. From there, they navigate back to the cargo lift Wes had activated earlier. The lift, still powered by the sled's ancient energy cell, carries them and their precious cargo upwards, back towards the relative safety of the hidden hangar bay.

Finally, the lift doors hiss open. The sight of the *Seventh Moon*, sitting silently in the dusty bay, is a welcome

Sera's voice suddenly crackles over the comms, the signal weak and laced with static, but her tone is urgent.***"...Wes? ...arina? Do you copy...? We have a... ...situation up here... Repeat, Team Skimmer has a... ...ontact..."*** The signal cuts out into a burst of static, then silence.

The journey back to the *Seventh Moon* took longer than expected but the added time was worth it considering the success they had in acquiring the main objective that they had set out to do.  Securing the two massive power converters that now rode along with them.  


As Marina expertly guided them back to the secret hanger bay where the *Moon*, their potential get away transport, was docked by their comms activated.   The other team was reaching out.  Apparently they were out of range of the makeshift setup they had arranged.   At least now they could hear.   He silently wondered how long they were trying to raise them.  They were having trouble and it counted like they had contact with something. He quickly wondered if the two droids' activation down in engineering set off others they may have been near.  


He looked back at Marina at the controls and Roric who was next to her watching their back.  His T visor, impassive as always, did little to hide his concern from Marina’s Force senses.  


**”Copy Sera.  Wes here.  What’s the sit rep?”**  As he spoke he reached out with his sense.  Past the bulkheads towards his teammates.  As he waited.   Hoping that the burst of static followed by the silence was as ominous as it sounded.

Wes extends his senses, pushing his awareness through the decks of the colossal derelict, searching for the familiar presences of his teammates. He feels for Sera and Echo, but the sheer mass of the ancient ship and the lingering, ambient Force-resonance from the reactor complex act like a thick, confusing fog. He can't get a clear lock on them. He senses the general direction of the bridge, far above, but their individual signatures are lost in the overwhelming silence and the ship's ancient, dormant energy.

His suit's comm system remains stubbornly silent. Sera's fragmented, urgent message is followed only by the low, steady hiss of static. There is no reply.

Marina, her hands still on the sled's controls, looks over at Wes, her own face a mask of concern. **"I can't raise them either,"** she reports, having tried to hail them on the fragile relay link. **"The connection's dead. That last burst... it sounded like they were cut off mid-sentence."**

**"Contact,"** Roric grunts from his position, his voice a low, gravelly rumble over the comms. **"That's what she said. Could be anything. Rival scavengers, more of those droids... or something else entirely."** He shifts his grip on his ion rifle, his posture becoming even more alert as he scans the shadows of the hidden hangar bay, as if expecting a threat to emerge from the darkness at any moment.

He looked at Roric as the gruff soldier spoke **”Contact with something.  The message didn’t sound dire but the status of the comm was less than desired.”**  He looked over to Marina.  She stood tall and he knew her strength.  He saw the lighted face from beneath the helmet's face shield.  With her being pregnant he knew she was tired though she’d never admit on mission especially over open comms.


He looked around the hanger bay and over to the *Moon.*  **”Marina, let’s get the converters ready to be loaded on to the Moon and look at the map the others took to the bridge.”**  He swung his gaze back to her.  **”I want to be on the move quickly.”**  He locked eyes with her as a look passed between them.  Both realizing that they may need to separate once more.  He reached out to her within the Force.     


He looked to his left at the *Moon.*  He didn’t like the idea that was forming but they both knew what was expected.  **”Ok we have two objectives.  First, team Skimmer has made some sort of contact on the bridge.  We don’t know what, with who or the current situation.  We need to get up there.  Second, getting our cargo loaded and making sure the *Moon* will get us back through the expanse and home to the Chu’unthor.”**  He spoke his next words carefully.  **”Do we have what’s needed to load onto the *Moon?*”**

Marina gives a sharp, decisive nod, her mind already shifting to the new, urgent set of problems. She immediately begins guiding the heavy repulsor sled towards the *Seventh Moon's* open cargo hatch.

**"The converters are ready to load, Wes,"** she confirms, her voice all business. **"But the *Moon* is still completely cold. We'll have to manually haul them aboard for now. The Ghtroc's own cargo loading ramp isn't powered."**


She looks up at him, her blue eyes holding a mixture of a mechanic's pragmatism and a partner's deep concern. **"The choice is stark, Wes. We can either focus all our efforts on the *Moon*, try to get it powered up and ready for a quick departure with the salvage we have, and hope Team Skimmer can handle themselves... or we abandon this ship for now, leave the converters here, and make a run for the bridge to support them, knowing we might be walking into a trap with no clear way to get back."**

She looks at him, her expression grim, the unspoken reality hanging between them. **"Wes... if Team Skimmer is in trouble, we're the only ones who can help them. But getting this salvage back to the *Chu'unthor* is critical for everyone. And... you're the only other pilot."**

Roric, having listened to the exchange, steps forward. **"Del-Fin,"** he says, his voice a low rumble. **"My job is security. If you're going to the bridge, you'll need a gun you can count on. Marina needs to stay here, get this bird ready to fly. That's the only way any of this matters. You and I go. We find your team."**

Wes met the gaze of Marina and Roric as they spoke.  Both had valid points.  Each team knew there was a risk in splitting up.  The three of them were lucky.  They had managed to get the salvage and back to the *Moon* with little trouble and a lot of luck.  


After a quick moment he nodded.  **”We need to find out what's going on with Team Skimmer.  I’m not leaving half the team behind without communications on what’s going on.  Marina, have the converters ready to load so it’s easy to do when we get back.  Start going over the *Moon* but don’t power her up.  Check systems but keep the power spikes low so we’re not drawing attention like we did in engineering.”**  Looking into her eyes he saw what anyone else would.  Determination and confidence but through their bond he felt a whole other gambit of emotions.  Emotions he felt himself.  They tore at him.   The idea of leaving the woman he loved and their unborn child didn’t sit well but there was no option.   These weren’t peaceful times.


He looked into Marina’s eyes.  The blue eyes that he had fallen for.  He hoped that their child had the same eyes.   Taking a couple steps he closed the distance between them and took one off her hands.  The connection was diminished due to the heavy gear they wore but it was still there.  **”I can't leave them behind.  We’ll be as quick as we can.  Cyar’ika Well move fast and be back before you know it.”**

Marina's gloved fingers tighten around Wes's, the diminished but still present tingle of their connection a small, grounding comfort. The thick material of their gloves doing little to dampen the flood of emotion she feels from him through their bond—his sense of duty, his fear for her, his love. She gives his hand a firm, resolute squeeze. She gives him a single, sharp nod, her expression a mask of professional resolve that can't entirely hide the deep well of concern in her eyes. **"I know, Wes,"** she says, her voice low but steady. **"I wouldn't expect you to. They're our people."**

She squeezes his hand back, a silent transfer of strength and trust. **"Go. Be careful. I'll have the converters prepped and run every pre-flight diagnostic I can without waking the *Shadow*. This ship will be as ready as I can possibly make it for when you get back."** She lets go, her hand falling away, a clear sign that the moment for personal connection is over and the mission is paramount. **"Now go. Roric's waiting."** Before he turns from her she gives him a small, reassuring smile. “**May the Force be with you.**”

Roric, who had been silently observing the exchange with the stoic patience of a veteran, gives a curt nod as Wes turns to him. He hefts his ion rifle, its bulk a reassuring presence in the dim, silent hangar. **"Ready when you are, Del-Fin,"** he grunts.

He smiled beneath his helmet.  He could feel the warmth, love and understanding from Marina.   Once again he was thankful for the mission to Fakir that brought them together.   He listened as she laid out what she would be doing when they left.  When she told them to get going he smiled and gave her hand a squeeze before she let go.  He stepped back but stopped when spoke.   He gave her a nod as he placed his hand on his blaster echoing her sentiment.  **May the Force be with you.”**  


With the parting words spoken he spun to Roric.  The gruff  soldier stood by silently as he witnessed the two lovers part.  A man of few words, Roric said he was ready to go.   


Giving the veteran soldier a nod he pointed to the seamless door.   **”Let's go see what has held up the rest of our team.  Let’s follow the route they took.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content: Roric grunts in acknowledgment. **"Copy. To the bridge."** He falls into step just behind Wes's shoulder, his heavy rifle held at a low ready, his gaze already sweeping the shadows of the hidden hangar bay as they move back towards the service corridor they'd used to find this place.

The two warriors, one a Mandalorian Jedi, the other a hardened veteran, leave the relative light of the *Seventh Moon* behind. They cross the dusty hangar bay, their suit lights cutting a path back to the heavy, sealed door they'd sliced open earlier. As the door hisses shut behind them, they are once again plunged into the claustrophobic, silent darkness of the *Veiled Shadow's* service tunnels.

Their HUDs glow with the route Echo had plotted for Team Skimmer – a winding, complex path that leads upwards, deck by deck, towards the command section. It's a long, tense journey ahead, and the silence from their teammates' comms is a heavy, ominous presence. They move with a quiet, deadly purpose, two armored figures against the immense, ancient silence of the ghost ship.

As they emerged into the service tuned beyond the secret hidden door Wes reached out with his senses.   The path looked clear based on the new map they both had loaded.   They were about to trace the other team's path.  **”Alright Roric.  Eyes and ears open.  I’ll also be keeping my Force senses open.  I’m guessing they reached the objective alright but something came up there.  I’m hoping the path there is clear but we won’t take any chances.  We’ll be as quick as possible while being methodical.”**  He looked at the veteran that he had come to trust and depend on.  Giving the man a nod he unhooked his lightsaber from his utility belt and set out on the path marked on his HUD.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
Wes takes point, his lightsaber unlit but held ready in his hand. Roric falls into step behind him, his own heavy rifle at a low ready, their suit lights cutting a path through the oppressive darkness of the maintenance corridor. They move with a quiet, practiced urgency, following the glowing route on their HUDs that traces Team Skimmer's path towards the bridge.

The journey upwards through the guts of the *Veiled Shadow* is a tense, claustrophobic ascent. They navigate a series of cramped corridors and then come to a vertical maintenance shaft, just as the map indicated. A rickety, ancient ladder is bolted to the far wall, disappearing into the blackness above.

As they begin their climb, their lights illuminate the first concrete sign of their teammates' passage. About ten meters up the shaft, Wes spots a discarded, spent power cell for a blaster carbine, half-buried in a nook thick with dust – undoubtedly Echo's. A few meters higher, his light catches a fresh, angry-looking scorch mark on the metal wall. The dispersal pattern is sharp, focused—a clear **ion scorch mark**, evidence that a directed energy weapon was fired here. It wasn't just a firefight; Team Skimmer engaged a droid in this very shaft.

The ladder groans under their combined weight, the ancient metal protesting every upward step. They are about twenty meters up, Roric just below Wes, when a sharp, sickening **CRACK** echoes in the shaft. The section of ladder Roric is gripping tears away from the wall with a shower of rusted bolts and metal fragments.
**"Lost my hold!"** Roric's voice is a sharp grunt of surprise over the comms as he lurches downwards, his heavy frame dropping into the abyss.

He falls only a meter or two before his training kicks in, his free hand lashing out and catching the twisted edge of a lower, still-secured rung. He hangs there, his heavy armor and gear a pendulum of potential disaster, the broken section of ladder clattering down into the darkness below him. The remaining ladder groans ominously under the sudden, violent shift in weight.

Leading the way up the ladder, evidence of team Skimmer was abundant.   A spent power cell and an ion scorch mark stood out and identified they encountered a droid.  


Looking up he saw that they still had a ways to go.   He was about to say something when the creaking sound of the metal ladder signaled compromise.   The sound coming from below him meant that Roric was in trouble.   Before he could react he saw the veteran soldier begin to fall.  A section of the ladder still in hand.   A second later Wes saw Roric snag a rung with his free hand.  There his fellow soldier swung freely.  The metal of the existing ladder continued to groan from the sharp jarring movement.   


There was no doubt that the combined weight of the two of them was close to if not double of the previous team that passed.  


**”Stang.  Hold on Roric.”**  Wes said urgently as he took three more steps up the ladder to get closer to where this section was anchored down.   


**”We’re clearly heavier than the others.   This may not hold the both of us all the way up.  Do you trust me?”**  He saw the man he had come to trust with his life give him a nod.  **”Alright. I’m going to grab you with the Force and send down a line.   I’m not sure how long that rung your on will hold out.”**


First he reached out to Roric.  He could feel the gruff soldiers' presence within the Force.  Focusing on the living presence he enveloped it with a telekinetic grip.  Once that was established he reached down and sent down a line from his utility belt’s liquid cable dispenser.  A back up line in case his concentration was broken.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content: Wes scrambles up the next few rungs, his own armored boots finding purchase on the groaning metal. He braces himself against the wall, one hand gripping the ladder, the other outstretched towards the precarious form of Roric dangling below.
**"I trust you,"** Roric grunts back over the comm, his voice strained as he maintains his one-handed grip on the bent, protesting rung.

Wes takes a deep, centering breath. He focuses his will, reaching out with the Force. A tangible, invisible grip envelops Roric, not lifting him, but steadying him, countering the pull of gravity and taking the immediate, critical strain off the damaged ladder and Roric's own arm. The ominous groaning from the remaining ladder supports lessens, though it does not cease entirely.

With Roric's fall momentarily arrested by his telekinetic hold, Wes quickly deploys the liquid cable dispenser from his utility belt. A thin, incredibly strong synth-cable snakes down through the darkness, its luminescent tip glowing softly.

Roric, feeling the slight, unnatural buoyancy of the Force supporting him, carefully releases his grip on the broken rung and snatches the descending cable with his free hand. He quickly secures it around his chest harness with a practiced, one-handed knot.

**"I'm secure on the line,"** he confirms. **"Ready to move."** With the safety line in place and Wes's Force-grip providing stability, the immediate crisis is averted. Roric begins the arduous process of finding new handholds on the less-damaged sections of the ladder, his weight now supported by both the synth-line and Wes's focused power.

Wes was looking upwards when he heard Roric confirm that he was on the line.  He slowly let out a breath of focused relief **”Copy.  Let’s move up farther and get off the compromised section of the ladder.  Keep ascending and I’ll maintain concentration in case anything gives.”**


Slowly and in coordination with Roric’s movement.  Wes moved only when getting confirmation from Roric that he had a secure hold and was moving upwards.  This kept the slack in the line at a minimum.  This way if the veteran soldier lost grip or the ladder gave way again the line would already be taunt.  It worked well in conjunction with the use of the Force as the sections of ladder that needed to support Roric didn’t need to support his entire weight.   


Slowly they climbed.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
The ascent becomes a slow, grueling, but steady rhythm of coordinated effort. Wes climbs a few rungs, then holds his position, his mind a focused point of telekinetic power, supporting a portion of Roric's considerable weight. Below, Roric moves with a grim determination, testing each rung before trusting it, his armored gloves scraping against the corroded metal, the synth-cable a reassuring tether in the darkness.

They pass the point where the ladder tore away, a jagged scar of metal against the shaft wall. The structure groans and shifts, but holds. They continue upwards, the only sounds the scrape of their boots, the hiss of their recyclers, and Roric's occasional, gruff "Moving" over the comms.

After what feels like an eternity, but is likely only a few minutes, Wes's helmet light illuminates a solid, grated platform above them – the top of the maintenance shaft. With a final burst of effort, he hauls himself onto the platform, the solid deck a welcome relief. He immediately turns, anchoring the liquid cable line and maintaining his Force grip.

A moment later, Roric's helmet appears over the edge, and he pulls himself up, his breathing heavy but controlled. He stands, disconnects the cable, and gives the shaft a final, disdainful look.

**"Compromised is an understatement,"** he grunts, his voice tight with the residual adrenaline. He gives Wes a single, sharp nod of profound, unspoken gratitude. **"Good work, Del-Fin. Let's not do that again."**

They stand in a new corridor, this one similar to the ones below – dark, silent, and coated in ancient dust. The immediate danger is past, but the scorch marks on the wall of the shaft are a stark reminder that they are following a path where others have already fought, and perhaps fallen.

Wes nodded and discarded the line as Roric thanked him and suggested they not do it again.  He nodded and let out a short laugh as he looked back down the hazardous climb they had just made.  **”Agreed.  We’re going to have to find another way back.”**  He took a deep breath after the strenuous exertion of physical and Force ability.  


Turning his head he looked down the new corridor that they stood in.  It was similar to all the other ones they had walked since arriving.  Although this one was different. It showed evidence of a recent firefight.   Their teammates fought here as well.  The only positing was that there were no bodies.  


**”Alright, the map showed they went on from here.  Let’s go.   The more I see what they’ve encountered the more I don’t like it.”**  He unhooked his lightsaber hilt from his belt and looked at Roric.  **”Let's keep following their path.”**  As he turned and started walking he opened the teams comms.   **”Team Skimmer, Wes here.  If you can hear me, what's the sit rep?”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
Wes keys his comm, sending his query into the silent corridors of the *Veiled Shadow*. The only reply is the soft, steady hiss of static. There is no response from Team Skimmer. The silence is heavy, unnerving.

He and Roric proceed down the corridor, their suit lights cutting a path through the oppressive darkness. They move with a heightened sense of caution, their weapons held at a low ready, their senses scanning for any hint of a threat. The passage is long and straight, lined with the same ancient, sealed maintenance panels as the others.

As they continue, a low, discordant **hum** begins to permeate the silence. It's not a sound their ears can pick up in the vacuum, but a vibration that travels through the deck plating into the soles of their boots, a strange, unsettling resonance that seems to make their teeth ache. At the same time, the lights on their suits begin to flicker erratically, and their HUDs fill with shimmering, green-tinged static. The static on their comms intensifies, punctuated by a low, discordant whine.

Wes feels a nauseating pressure build in the Force, a disorienting, chaotic wave of psychic noise that makes it difficult to concentrate. It feels like standing too close to a massive, ancient, and malfunctioning engine, the sheer power of it overwhelming his senses.

Roric stops, his head tilting as if trying to shake water from his ear. **"What in the blazes...?"** he grunts, his voice tight. **"My HUD is glitching. Getting intermittent power warnings to my rifle's targeting laser."** He raises his rifle, and the red laser dot flickers and dies before coming back on. **"Feels... like the whole corridor is vibrating. You feeling this, Del-Fin?"**
The corridor ahead seems to twist and warp, the disorienting field playing tricks on their perception. Pushing forward will be a test of pure will and focus.

**”Yeah my HUD is also glitching but it’s more than that.”**  He saw the look that Roric gave him and he further explained.  He shook his head as if trying to reorient himself.   **”That resonance and vibration we can feel through the deckplating,”**  He paused to point down even though he knew Roric understood what he was getting at.  **”It is also happening in the Force but it magnified.   It’s dizzying and overwhelming, making it hard to concentrate.”**  He said through a groan like he had a migraine.   **”Something up here is in tune with the Force and the scale of it is immense.”**


He turned to look down the corridor that stretched on before them.   The rest of their team was down there somewhere and despite what he felt he was going to reach them.  


He took a breath and closed his eyes centering himself drawing in his presence.   He wasn’t sure if it would help with the overwhelming pressure of the Force in the area.  It was all he could think of at the moment.   


Letting his breath out he looked at Roric.   **”Lets push forward.  With the oppressive Force presence I’m not sure I’ll be able to sense danger.”**  He gave Roric a nod and started with a step towards the oppressive Force presence.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content: Wes takes a centering breath, drawing on his Jedi training. He wraps himself in a tight, protective bubble of will, pushing back against the crushing, chaotic resonance of the corridor. The overwhelming psychic noise doesn't vanish, but it recedes, becoming a manageable background roar rather than an all-consuming scream in his mind. The nausea subsides, and his focus returns, sharp and clear. He feels a sense of control, a small island of calm in a turbulent sea of the Force.

Roric, however, is not so fortunate. He grunts, taking a step forward, but then stumbles, his armored hand bracing against the vibrating wall. **"Kriff..."** he mutters, his voice strained over the comm. **"Head's spinning. Everything's... shifting."** The intense EM field and the low-frequency vibrations are playing havoc with his inner ear and his suit's stabilization systems. He's still on his feet, still moving forward, but his movements are sluggish, his aim unsteady, clearly fighting off a wave of profound vertigo.

The two of them press on, a strange pair in the flickering, static-filled corridor. Wes moves with a newfound, focused grace, his senses clear and sharp, while Roric plods grimly beside him, his every step a visible effort of will against the disorienting assault on his senses. The corridor stretches on, the oppressive hum a constant, maddening presence.

Wes felt the chaotic swirls and eddies of the Force that were assaulting him diminish.   As he pulled in his presence it felt like he had thrown a heavy cloak over himself.  The outside psychic noise that was trying to beat him down was pushed back.  It was still there but his own force of will seemed to push the outside forces back.   


They hadn’t moved far when Wes looked back.  He could see Roric was struggling.  The gruff soldier walked with a hand along the wall for balance.   The rifle that was always at the ready hung low.  He would have felt his companions distress had he not drawn in his own senses.   


Wes stopped and moved to the soldiers side.   With his left hand he placed it upon Roric’s armored shoulder.  **”I’m sorry Ror, I should have made sure you were alright.  Let me see if I can help.”**  He said as he wondered if he could.  He thought about an earlier training about pushing his senses outward.  He wondered if he could do what he just did for himself but extend the protective bubble he made for himself to also include Roric.  Not knowing what waited for them up ahead they both needed to be free of any hindrance.   


**”Hold on.   I haven’t tried this before.”**  He said over comms as he used his own ability to also protect his friend.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
Wes places a steadying hand on Roric's shoulder, a gesture of support in the disorienting corridor. He focuses his will, attempting to extend the protective bubble of calm he'd created for himself to encompass the struggling soldier. He pushes outwards with the Force, trying to impose his own sense of order on the chaos battering Roric.

But it's like trying to shield someone from a hurricane with a thin sheet of glass. The psychic noise and electromagnetic chaos are too powerful, too pervasive. His mental shield, a product of internal discipline, cannot be projected to block a physical and environmental effect on another person. He feels his own focus strain, the chaotic "whispers" pressing back at the edges of his concentration, threatening to overwhelm him again if he spreads his defense too thin.

Roric grunts, shaking his head as if to clear it, but the effort seems to make the vertigo worse. **"It's no good, Del-Fin,"** he rasps, his voice strained. **"It's... in my helmet systems. My... my balance is shot."** The disorienting field is a physical assault on his senses and his gear, not just a mental one. He's still on his feet, but his movements are clumsy, his reactions clearly dulled by the overwhelming sensory input.

Frustration settled in when he wasn’t able to do for his companion that he wasn’t able to do for himself.  It was something he would have to ask Master Davion about when they got back.  He watched as the gruff soldier grunted  and shook his head, Roric tried to stand taller but once more the vertigo took hold.  **”It’s not just your helmet systems.  It’s the Force.  It’s as if there’s a storm raging.  It’s all I can do to keep myself from subcumming to the effects.”**


Seeing that he couldn’t help him, Wes pulled his own protection back to shore up his defenses to the onslaught of Force eddies that continued to buffet them.   **”Okay side by side we go.   This way we can help cover each other.”**. Wes shifted slightly to stand next to Roric.  **”Put a hand on my shoulder to steady yourself.  We’ll get there together.”** 


Looking ahead he waited for Roric to reach out before moving.   Again he tried to raise the other team.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content: Roric gives a grim, single nod. The offer of a physical anchor is a practical solution he can understand. He reaches out, his heavy armored glove clamping onto Wes's shoulder pauldron, the grip firm and steadying. **"Alright, Del-Fin,"** he grunts, his voice still tight with effort. **"Let's move."**

Wes keys his comm again, sending out another call. The only reply is the same maddening, discordant hum that fills their helmets.

Together, they begin a slow, arduous shuffle down the flickering, static-filled corridor. Wes acts as a guide and a living railing, his own senses clear and focused within his Force-shield, while Roric leans on him, his every step a battle against the overwhelming vertigo and the glitching readouts of his helmet's display. The journey is agonizingly slow, a testament to sheer, stubborn grit.

After what feels like an eternity, the oppressive hum begins to lessen. The static on their HUDs clears, the flickering lights of the corridor steady, and the nauseating pressure in the Force recedes. They have passed through the field.

Roric lets out a long, ragged breath and releases his grip on Wes's shoulder, standing on his own, though still swaying slightly. **"Kriff... that was... unpleasant,"** he manages, shaking his head one last time to clear the last of the dizziness.

They stand at the end of the long corridor. Before them is a final, heavy blast door, identical to the ones they've encountered before. This one, however, is not pristine. It is scarred with fresh, angry-looking blaster marks. On the floor lies a discarded blaster carbine—Echo's.

The blast door to the bridge access corridor is sealed shut. And standing guard before it, its back to them, is the source of the disturbance. It's a tall, slender, humanoid droid, its polished dark chassis glowing with an eerie, pale blue light from its joints. A distorted, shimmering aura of Force energy surrounds it. One of its arms is a long, elegant energy lance, currently inert.

And lying slumped against the wall nearby, her form still and unmoving, is Sera.

emoji.url: Galactic Campaigns - SW_ Whispers in the Void - wv_ch3_the-karthon-gamble [1375296954300366910].json_Files/1f389-5c7383b4dc541d96.svg

content:
The trek down the hallway felt like an eternity.   Wes was continually buffeted by the Force storm.   He had no other words for it other than that.  If he hadn’t come he wasn’t sure Roric would have made it alone.   It also cemented the worry that the other team was in trouble.  That alone forced him to keep his concentration to reach the other team.  It was his decision to separate in order to accomplish both goals.   


Finally he felt the buffeting effects of the Force begin to lessen.  **”We’re getting past the field.   Just a bit farther.”**  


A few moments more the hum had hounded them had lessened and the nauseating physical effects of the Force receded as they finally passed through the field.  It wasn’t just felt physically it also showed in his armor’s systems.   His HUD that had been a color burst of static was coming back to a stable condition.    **”We’re through.   The debilitating effects should resolve quickly.”**  He paused for a second before adding.  **”I think.”**  


He felt Roric remove his hand from his pauldron as the veteran soldier stood as if stretching the effects of what they went through out of his body.  As his companion worked though the now receding effects for the Force storm Wes looked down the corridor.  What he saw stopped him cold.  


At the end of the corridor he saw another blast door.  This one battle scorched and a discarded blaster rifle lay up against it.   He recognized it but he didn’t see its owner.  Echo was nowhere to be seen.  He did see two other things.  One ran his blood cold.  Sera laid still slumped against the wall.  The other now had his full focus.   


Wes eyed the droid.  He had never seen its like before as pale blue light emanated from its joints.  He keyed his comms with Roric.   **”Ror.   Dead ahead.  Take it slow.  Be ready but don’t engage just yet.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content: Slowly he walked towards Sera.  His lightsaber held at the ready but still unlit.  Now that they were close he tried to raise team *Skimmer* on the comms.   **”Team skimmer, it’s Wes.   Anyone picking up this transmission?  Voice only don’t move.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
Wes's voice crackles over the comms, met only by the faint hiss of static and the low hum of their own suit systems. There is no reply from Sera, Echo, or Kael. The silence is a heavy, damning answer.

As Wes and Roric advance cautiously down the corridor, the scene of carnage comes into sharper focus. The two "janitor" droids are mangled heaps, one clearly ripped apart by a powerful explosive, the other riddled with precise ion burns. Sera is slumped against the bulkhead, her helmet still on, but she is utterly still, showing no signs of consciousness.

The humanoid droid stands over the twitching form of a third janitor droid. It remains with its back to them, its attention focused on the inert machine at its feet. The pale blue light from its joints pulses with a slow, steady rhythm, and the disorienting  aura is a palpable pressure in the Force, though not as overwhelming as the field they just traversed. It seems unaware of their approach. Yet. A fourth droid stands idling on the otherside side of the door, powered down.

Roric moves with a soldier's practiced silence, his heavy boots making no sound on the dusty deck. He raises his ion rifle, the targeting laser a steady red dot painting the back of the Custodian's humanoid chassis. He looks to Wes, awaiting the signal, his expression grim and focused.

The moment is stretched taut. They have the element of surprise. Sera is down, Echo is missing, and a powerful, unknown hostile stands just meters away, its purpose and capabilities a complete mystery.

Wes moved closer to Sera.   His left hand was out to his side held flat as he pumped it down for Roric to take it slow.  There was a fight here but between who and what.   Sera was here and he moved slowly towards his fallen teammate.   He thought about calling out again over comms but didn’t.  Echo and Kael were still nowhere to be found.  **”The blue droid is giving off the same type of resonance we dealt with walking down the corridor but not as overwhelming.”**  He paused for a second as he knelt down.  **”The blue one could be friendly considering how it’s standing over the janitor droid.”**  His voice trailed off.  


Keeping his eye on the two droids he moved closer to Sera.  He didn’t take his eyes off the two potential hostiles.  He wanted to get to Sera and see if she was still alive.   He reached out to her with the Force.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
As Wes reaches out with the Force towards Sera, he feels a spark of life. She is alive, her consciousness suppressed, likely by a powerful stun blast or a neuro-shock effect. Her life signs are stable, but she is deeply unconscious.

Just as he confirms this, the Custodian droid makes its move.

It wasn't unaware. It was waiting.

With a speed that seems impossible for its size, the Custodian pivots, its single, glowing blue optic locking directly onto Wes. The humming, disorienting aura flares, washing over them with a fresh wave of psychic pressure and static. The energy lance integrated into its arm flares to life with a low, menacing hum, its tip glowing with the same pale blue light as its joints.

It takes a single, deliberate step forward, ignoring the inert janitor droid at its feet. It raises its lance, not in an immediate attack, but in a clear, challenging posture. A distorted, multi-tonal voice, like grinding metal and whispered echoes, emanates from its vocabulator, crackling over their comms.
**"...[STATIC]...-f-finder... detected... Un...-n-authorized... bio-signatures... must... be... [STATIC]...-purged-..."**

author.username: The Custodian ▕█ HP; 0 CT • ▕█ SR; 10 DR
author.global_name: The Custodian ▕█ HP; 0 CT • ▕█ SR; 10 DR

userName: The Custodian ▕█ HP; 0 CT • ▕█ SR; 10 DR

content:
Gently he reached out to her with the Force.  It was then his senses told him what his eyes couldn’t.  She was alive but out cold from some sort of effect.  **”She’s alive…”** Was all he was able to get out before the droid moved and its energy lance activated.   Wes couldn’t help but notice that the lance flowed with the same blue light that emanated from its joints.  It took a step forward and its weapon was raised.  Not into a full attack posture but it was ready.  


As its single eye that glowed blue was fixed upon him Wes felt another wave of psychic energy emanate out.   Thankfully he hadn’t dropped his defenses and let the debilitating energy flowed around him. 


Conscious of his actions he stood slowly and kept his weapon hand low but angled so that he could activate his weapon letting the blade appear between him and the droid.  He held his free hand out to Roric.  A gesture to hold as their comms crackled.  This droid was the first one to actually speak even if it was garbled. Several words made sense but one stood out.   *Finder.*  


**”Ror hold!  Be ready.”**  He said with urgency as he stood slowly.   **”It said ‘finder’.  Pathfinder or Wayfinder it’s too coincidental.”**  He lowered his hand that he had held out to Roric.  


He stood still for a couple seconds before deciding on a course of action.  Gently he reached out to the droid in the Force.   It seemed to be the source of the chaotic Force storm they just passed through and the same effects surged again just now.   He had never heard of a droid being able to use the Force.   He wasn't even sure how that was possible but if the presence in the engineering could sense what he was maybe this droid could too.   Maybe it was just defending the ship.  


**”Pathfinder.  You detect the Pathfinder?”**  He said calmly

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕█92/92 HP 0 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content: The Custodian's glowing blue optic remains fixed on Wes, seemingly processing his calm words. The disorienting aura continues to pulse, a psychic and electronic shriek that presses in on them. For a moment, the droid remains still, its energy lance held in its challenging posture. Then, its distorted, metallic voice crackles over their comms again, its words laced with an ancient, implacable logic.

**"...Pathfinder... resonance... confirmed,"** it grinds out. **"...Contamination... detected. Protocol is absolute. The Path... must... be... [STATIC]...-protected-..."**

With a final, chilling burst of static, its posture shifts from challenge to attack.

author.username: The Custodian ▕█ HP; 0 CT • ▕█ SR; 10 DR
author.global_name: The Custodian ▕█ HP; 0 CT • ▕█ SR; 10 DR

userName: The Custodian ▕█ HP; 0 CT • ▕█ SR; 10 DR

content:
The Custodian lunges forward with a speed that belies its size, its movements unnaturally fluid. It closes the distance to Wes in a blur of dark metal and pale blue light. The energy lance at the end of its arm hums with power as it thrusts forward, aiming a devastating blow at the Mandalorian Jedi.

Wes brings his green lightsaber up in a desperate attempt to block, but the sheer force and precision of the ancient droid's attack is overwhelming. The energy lance slips past his guard, its glowing tip slamming into his beskar chest plate with incredible force. The blow is massive, leaving a blackened, superheated crater on his Mandalorian armor.

Wes stood there waiting as the droid apparently thought about his words.  He listened as the droid spoke initially thinking that it was going to work.  Then the droid attacked with a speed and ferocity he had never seen before.  


With a loud grunt he was pushed back into the bulkhead by the droid's ferocious attack.  He was surprised by the speed of the droid's movement and attack.  But it didn’t make any sense.  If he heard it correctly it was protecting the path from the Pathfinder.  Himself.  


He took a handed grip upon the hilt of his lightsaber knowing he was going to need the added strength to block the droid's powerful attacks.  He altered his position into a full defensive stance to parry the next attack when it came.  He didn’t want to destroy it.  He hoped there could be a way to alter its course.  


With a painful grunt he spoke loudly and with authority.   His green blade cast emerald shadows in the area and glinted off the droid's chassis.  **”Yes I’m the Pathfinder.  Why protect the path from the pathfinder?”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕▅64/92 HP 1 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕▅64/92 HP 1 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕▅64/92 HP 1 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
Roric, seeing Wes take a heavy hit, doesn't hesitate. He adjusts his aim. **"Eat this, you bucket of bolts!"** he snarls.

The ion rifle cracks, and a brilliant blue bolt of energy slams squarely into the Custodian's chest. The droid shudders, its glowing blue joints flickering erratically as the ion charge courses through its ancient systems. Its energy shield visibly wavers and weakens under the assault.

Just as the Custodian is hit, the two droids finally lurch into action. Their photoreceptors snap to a hostile red, and they scuttle forward with a grinding of ancient servos, their weaponized appendages whirring to life.

Guardian Droid 1 charges towards Roric, its plasma cutter humming with intense heat. It lunges, swiping its glowing tool in a wide, clumsy arc. Roric, ever the veteran, takes a quick step back, and the superheated cutter slices through empty air, inches from his chest plate.

Guardian Droid 2 scuttles towards Wes. It brings its own plasma cutter around, attempting to strike at the armored Jedi. The attack is slow, telegraphed, and Wes easily deflects it with a slight shift of his body, the attack passing harmlessly.

The Custodian seems to ignore the ion blast that just weakened its shields, its glowing blue optic still fixed solely on Wes. It takes another deliberate, almost graceful step forward, raising its energy lance once more. Its distorted, metallic voice crackles over the comms, a chillingly calm response to Wes's question.

**"...The Path... is... knowledge. The Pathfinder... is the... key. A key... must not... be... contaminated..."**

It lunges again, its energy lance a blur of blue light aimed directly at Wes.

author.username: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10
author.global_name: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

userName: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

content:
The fight was now full on.   Roric was now engaged with a cleaner droid.   Wes now had two attackers upon him.  The custodian and a cleaner droid.  In the tight quarters the clearer droid couldn’t get the angle and its plasma cutter swung wide leaving a harmless red trail of light.


As before the custodian answered but it didn’t make any sense.  The Pathfinder was the key but the key must not be contaminated.  


He quickly sidestepped  to keep the cleaner between him and the custodian who was the bigger threat.  He struck quickly looking to sever the plasma cutter from the appendage.   


**”The Pathfinder and his allies must not be harmed.  I am not contaminated.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ▕▅64/92 HP 1 CT • 8 FP 3 DP
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ▕▅64/92 HP 1 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

userName: Wes Del-Fin ▕▅64/92 HP 1 CT • 8 FP 3 DP

content:
The Custodian's disorienting aura pulses with renewed intensity. Both Wes and Roric feel the wave of psychic static slam into their minds. Their vision flickers with phantom data streams, and a wave of vertigo washes over them, making it difficult to focus. The unsettling effect takes hold, a dissonant hum clouding their thoughts and reflexes.

The Custodian glides forward, its energy lance humming. It seems to process Wes's words, its blue optic flickering for a moment.

author.username: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10
author.global_name: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

userName: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

content:
**"...Statement... logged. Contamination... protocol... remains... absolute..."**

Its logic unwavering, it lunges, thrusting its energy lance in a powerful, precise strike. Wes, having taken a full defensive stance, anticipates the move. He brings his green lightsaber up, the blade catching the Custodian's lance with a brilliant shower of spaTherks and a sharp **CRACKLE**. The force of the blow is immense, but Wes holds his ground, successfully deflecting the attack.

author.username: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10
author.global_name: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

userName: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

content: Wes deftly sidesteps, trying to create a better angle as he counters. His lightsaber flashes, a swift, targeted strike aimed at the Custodian itself. Hampered by the disorienting aura, his attack is a fraction of a second too slow, and his blade slices through empty air as the ancient droid makes a minute, impossibly fast adjustment to its stance.

Roric, fighting through the wave of vertigo from the aura, adjusts his aim, targeting the Custodian once more. He fires his ion rifle, but the shot goes wide, the blue energy bolt dissipating harmlessly against the far bulkhead. The disorienting field is clearly making precise shots a significant challenge.Roric, fighting through the wave of vertigo from the aura, adjusts his aim, targeting the Custodian once more. He fires his ion rifle, but the shot goes wide, the blue energy bolt dissipating harmlessly against the far bulkhead. The disorienting field is clearly making precise shots a significant challenge

Guardian Droid 1 scuttles forward and brings its plasma cutter down in a vicious, cleaving arc toward Roric. The superheated tool slams into Roric's armor, sending a shower of sparks and leaving a deep, molten scar across his chest plate. Roric grunts in pain, staggering back a step from the force of the blow.

Guardian Droid 2 lunges forward towards Wes. It brings its own plasma cutter around in a swift, hissing strike. 
Just as the superheated tool is about to make contact, Wes's free hand snaps up, palm open. He reaches out, not with his blade, but with the Force itself. A shimmering, barely visible field of energy materializes in the air before him. The plasma cutter slams into this invisible barrier with a loud VWOOMP and a shower of brilliant, white-hot sparks. The energy of the attack is absorbed, dissipated harmlessly into the Force. Wes stands his ground, untouched by the blow, the green blade of his lightsaber still held at the ready.

The Custodian's disorienting aura pulses again, but having felt its effects before, Wes manages to steel his mind against the worst of the psychic static. Roric, however, is not so lucky; the wave of vertigo washes over him again, clouding his senses.

The Custodian, its logic implacable, once again identifies Wes as the primary threat. It glides forward, its energy lance held low, then thrusts upwards in a powerful, precise attack aimed at bypassing Wes's guard.

author.username: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10
author.global_name: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

userName: The Custodian HP▕█; CT 0 • SR▕▅; DR 10

content: Wes reacts instantly, his green lightsaber a blur of motion. He brings the blade down, meeting the Custodian's lance with a sharp **CRACKLE** of energy. Sparks fly as he expertly deflects the main force of the blow, the energy lance skittering harmlessly off his humming blade. He holds his ground, the attack successfully parried.

**”Roric!”**  Wes yelled as he saw his friend take a brutal hit from the cleaner droid's plasma cutter.  The debilitating effects from the Custodian was once again flaring causing them both problems.   They were at a severe disadvantage.   


With a two handed grip he held the Custodian's energy lance away with his own weapon.   The green blade of his lightsaber crackled against the lance holding it at bay.  With his team, no friends, hurt and missing he couldn’t try to reason with the Custodian anymore.  **"I can't allow you to interfere anymore.  The lives of my family and friends as well as the Jedi Order depend on us getting back.”**


With strength gained from Mandalorian and Jedi training Wes applied leverage with his lightsaber and pushed his opponent's weapon back.   He needed to change the tide of their battle.  It had the possibility of leaving him exposed but Roric was in trouble.  


With the energy lance pushed back he reached out with one hand towards the Cleaner droid that was on Roric.   Like before he gripped it with the Force and hurdled it at the second cleaner droid.  Hoping that the Force and some luck was still on their side.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Wes's voice rings with grim determination and with a surge of strength, he uses his lightsaber to shove the Custodian's energy lance aside, creating a momentary opening. But as he shifts his focus, dropping his guard to reach out with the Force, the ancient droids seize the opportunity.

The Custodian's blue optic flashes. It pivots with impossible speed, its now-free energy lance whipping around in a vicious counter-attack. The glowing tip slams into Wes's side, the force of the blow staggering him. At the same moment, the janitor droid beside him (GD2) swipes its plasma cutter, but Wes, reeling from the first hit, instinctively recoils, and the superheated tool misses by a hair's breadth.

Ignoring the searing pain in his side, Wes extends his hand towards the other janitor droid (GD1) that is menacing Roric.

An invisible, crushing force envelops GD1. With a shriek of tortured metal, the droid is torn from its feet and hurtled across the antechamber like a piece of scrap. It smashes with incredible violence into its twin, GD2. The impact is a catastrophic explosion of tangled limbs, shattered plating, and sparking, severed conduits. The two janitor droids collapse together in a heap of inert, smoking ruin, their red photoreceptors extinguished for good.

Staggering from the hit he took and the disorienting aura, Roric sees Wes clear the immediate threat. Seizing the opening, he brings his ion rifle to bear on the now-exposed Custodian. **"Payback time!"** he snarls.

The rifle cracks, and the ion bolt strikes the Custodian squarely. The droid's already weakened energy shield flares violently, sputters, and then collapses with a loud **POP**, leaving its armored chassis fully exposed.

Keeping his eyes on the Custodian, Wes heard Roric’s exclamation.  A split second later he saw the blue ball of ion energy impact the droid's shields.   The accurate shot caused a violent reaction on the droid's shields resulting in a satisfying popping noise.  Wes smiled as he saw the shimmer of the overloaded shield fades away.  


**”Solid hit!  Its defenses are down.”**  He said through gritted teeth as he held his left side with his forearm.   The hit he had just taken was hard.  If he hadn’t been armored he felt it would have taken him out of the fight.   **”Circling to keep it between us.”**


With an audible grunt he once again took a two handed grip upon the hilt of his weapon.   The green blade cast shadows across the custodian and himself.  With the Custodian too rigid in its directives Wes was done talking.   He did as he said and circled to the right, towards the two cleaner droids that were now a piled heap of metal against the wall.   Feinting an attack once to keep its attention focused on him.   Roric’s rifle was effective against the droids and Wes meant to provide him an opportunity.   


Before the droid was directly between them he made his move.  With a surge of the Force he attacked.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The Custodian's disorienting aura pulses once more. The wave of psychic static washes over both Wes and Roric, their senses already frayed from the ongoing battle. The disorienting effect takes hold, clouding their focus and reflexes for the coming crucial moments.

The Custodian, its shields now gone, recalculates its tactical priorities. It pivots, its movements still unnaturally smooth despite the damage it has weathered, and brings its energy lance to bear on Wes. It lunges, the glowing tip of the lance aimed in a devastating thrust.

However, hampered by the very aura it projects, or perhaps thrown off by Wes's preparatory movements, the attack is a fraction of a second too slow. Wes sidesteps, and the energy lance hisses through the empty air where he had just been standing.

Wes grits his teeth against the pain in his side and the disorienting hum in his head. He circles to the right, his movements a feint, drawing the Custodian's attention. Then, with a surge of power, he lunges.

He pours his focus, his pain, and his sheer determination into the attack, channeling the Force directly into his strike. His green lightsaber becomes a blinding arc of energy. The blade connects with the Custodian's armored chassis with a deafening **SHREEEENK** of protesting, superheated metal. The powerful, Force-augmented blow carves a massive, molten canyon across the droid's torso, severing primary power conduits and shattering internal mechanisms. The Custodian staggers back, its blue optic flickering wildly, its entire frame convulsing as smoke and sparks erupt from the grievous wound. It is critically damaged.

Seeing the devastating blow Wes landed, Roric takes the opportunity to finish recovering, shaking his head to clear the last of the disorienting effects. He brings his ion rifle up, aiming for a finishing shot, but in the chaotic aftermath of Wes's attack, his shot goes wide, impacting the far wall with a harmless sizzle.

After his strike he took a step back from the Custodian.  Smoke rose as sparks erupted out from the deep diagonal gash his lightsaber made along the droid's chest.  His strike was true and the droid was staggered.   The flickering optic was evidence that he had struck critical systems.  Despite the devastating strike it was still upright and dangerous.   Seeing Roric ready to shoot he took a half step back.   As the seasoned veteran fired the custodian lurched awkwardly and the shot went wide.   


Still a threat, Wes advanced taking an angled approach.  With speed born out of training and physical ability he struck at the droid again.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The Custodian's aura pulses again. The wave of psychic static washes over Wes, but he manages to keep his focus, shaking off the worst of the disorienting effect. Roric, however, is not so fortunate. The chaotic energy slams into his senses, and the world seems to tilt and shimmer around him, his concentration shattered.

Smoke and sparks pour from the grievous wound on the Custodian's chassis. Its movements are now jerky, erratic. Ignoring the damage, it raises its energy lance, its logic fixated on its primary target. It lunges, thrusting the lance in a desperate, powerful strike.

Wes, his senses clear, brings his free hand up, palm forward. A shimmering field of Force energy materializes just in time, intercepting the lance's tip. With a loud **VWOOMP**, the attack's energy is absorbed and dissipated, the blow negated completely.

author.username: The Custodian HP▕▃; CT 1 • SR▕ ; DR 10
author.global_name: The Custodian HP▕▃; CT 1 • SR▕ ; DR 10

userName: The Custodian HP▕▃; CT 1 • SR▕ ; DR 10

content: Seeing his attack thwarted, Wes presses his advantage, stepping in with a swift, angled strike of his own. But the Custodian, even crippled, is dangerously unpredictable. It lurches awkwardly, and Wes's green blade hisses through empty air.

Fighting through the disorienting haze of the aura, Roric sees the opening Wes has created. **"Stay down!"** he barks.

He fires his ion rifle. The blue bolt of energy strikes the critically damaged Custodian dead-center in its already wounded torso. The droid convulses violently as the ion charge courses through its ravaged systems, amplifying the existing damage. More sparks erupt, and a section of its chest plating is blown outward, clattering to the floor. The droid is still standing, but just barely.

The Custodian's aura pulses one last time, a final, desperate wave of psychic static. The disorienting energy washes over both Wes and Roric, clouding their thoughts and making the air shimmer.

Critically damaged and sparking violently, the ancient droid raises its energy lance. Its movements are slow, grinding, its blue optic flickering like a dying star. With the last of its strength, it lunges forward, aiming a final, desperate thrust at Wes.

Wes, fighting through the disorienting haze, brings his lightsaber up. The green blade meets the blue energy lance with a final, brilliant **CRACKLE** of energy. He holds the parry for a moment, the two blades locked, before shoving the Custodian's weapon aside. The attack is blocked.

Seeing the Custodian's final, desperate lunge deflected, Wes seizes the opening. He steps in, past the sputtering energy lance, and drives his green lightsaber deep into the droid's already mangled torso. The blade hisses as it punches through armor and ancient circuitry, and the Custodian shudders violently.
**"This ends now!"** Roric yells, stepping forward and firing his ion rifle one last time at point-blank range.

The ion bolt slams into the critically wounded droid. The Custodian's internal systems, already shredded by Wes's lightsaber, cascade into a final, catastrophic failure. The pale blue light in its joints and single optic flashes brightly, then extinguishes with a sound like a dying breath.

The ancient droid freezes for a half-second, a statue of dark, ruined metal. Then, with a final groan of protesting servos, it collapses, crashing to the deck plating with a deafening, final **CLANG**.

A profound silence falls over the antechamber, broken only by the hum of the team's suit systems and the faint, distant thrum of the *Veiled Shadow's* dormant power. 

The battle is over.

Wes stood there, faced off with the droid.  The green blade of his weapon angled out between the foe.   What was once an ancient droid that somehow was in tune with the Force was now a smoking mess of ruined metal.   

As he closed down his weapon the green shadows it casted diminished.  He stood there for a second as he shook his head at the waste.   **”Its protocols lead to its destruction.  We could have helped each other.”**  He said aloud but not directed at anyone in particular as he turned towards Sera.   


In seconds he was at the Zeltron's side already reaching out to her within the Force.   **”Sera!”**  He said as he placed a hand on her armored shoulder.  “Sera, it's Wes and Roric.  The threat is dealt with.   Wake up.”**  He gently moved her shoulder.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content: As Wes places his hand on Sera's shoulder, he can feel her life signs through the Force—steady, but deep in unconsciousness. His gentle shake has no effect. She remains slumped against the bulkhead, completely unresponsive.

Roric approaches, his ion rifle now held at a low ready. He kneels, his suit light playing over Sera's form. **"Stun blast,"** he grunts, his voice a low rumble. **"Looks like a heavy one. She's out cold, but her suit's diagnostics are still green. She's alive."** He glances at the sealed blast door to the bridge, then back at Wes. **"Whatever hit her, it was probably meant to incapacitate, not kill. They took Echo, stunned Sera. Hostage situation, maybe? Or they just needed one of them."** His pragmatic, tactical mind is already piecing together the grim possibilities.

Wes nodded as the Roric spoke.  He removed his hand from Sera’s shoulder at the veterans soldiers assessment of Sera.  **”Stunned the tech, and took the scout but where’s the pilot?”**  He looked at Roric as he reached for his medkit.  


It was a standard medkit.  The no frills kind but it had a chance of maybe helping Sera regain consciousness.   **”How’s your field medicine Ror?”**  He offered the kit over.  **”Fixing people up wasn’t always a strong suit of mine.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Roric takes the offered medpac, his armored gloves surprisingly deft as he opens it. **"It's been a while, but the basics stick with you,"** he says, his voice a low grumble. He kneels beside Sera and begins a quick but thorough assessment, his light scanning the readings on her suit's display.

**"Vitals are stable but suppressed,"** he reports, his tone all business. **"Looks like a neuro-shock charge, not a standard stun. Powerful enough to knock her out, but probably not permanently damaging."** He selects a broad-spectrum counter-agent hypospray from the medpac. With a practiced motion, he finds the injection port on Sera's suit and administers the dose. **"This should help counteract it. Now we wait. Could be minutes, could be longer."**

He looks up from his work, his gaze moving from the sealed bridge door to the corridor they just came from. **"As for the pilot... you're assuming they went back to the *Skimmer*. Echo and Sera were the advance team. If they hit this... droid... and the fight went south fast, they might have never made it back to Kael. Echo could have been captured right here, and Kael might not even know we have a problem yet."**

He looks back at Wes, the grim reality of their situation clear in his voice. **"We're cut off, Del-Fin. And we've got one team member down, one missing, and a sealed blast door between us and the last place they were seen."**

Wes handed the medkit over to Roric and did what he could to help.  He watched the experienced soldier administer the hydrospray into the suits port.  When he said it was time to wait he nodded and stood.   


As he walked to the door Ror continued to talk about the situation and he had a good point.  Kael could still be with their ship and has no idea of what’s going on.   It made sense.  Going back to the ship would take time and team ’Skimmer’ possibly skipped picking up the pilot.  


**”Agreed the situation isn’t good.   Let’s not rush in.”**  He pointed at the door.  **”Give the stims a moment to work.   The interference that droid was causing in the Force is gone.  I can reach out past this door.   If Echo is in there or nearby I’ll be able to sense her.”**  He placed the palm of his gloved and armored hand to the bridge's blast door.  Opening himself up once again to the Force he pushed his presence past the armored blast doors into the bridge.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
As Wes places his palm against the cold, scarred metal of the bridge blast door, he closes his eyes and extends his senses. The overwhelming psychic static of the Custodian is gone, leaving the normal, ambient Force-resonance of the ancient ship. He pushes his awareness through the thick durasteal, seeking any sign of life, any familiar presence.

He finds her.

The impression is clear, sharp, a bright spark of determined, anxious life in the silent void of the bridge beyond: Echo is in there. She's alive, conscious, and her emotional state is a tense mixture of hyper-vigilance, controlled fear, and fierce resolve. She's not alone. He can't get a clear sense of who or what is with her, only a confusing, cold impression – not the emptiness of a droid, but not the warmth of a normal living being either. It's a strange, unsettling presence, and it's focused entirely on the bridge's main console.

As he pulls back from the door, his eyes open and his helmet's light catches a detail he had overlooked. Etched hastily into the dusty floor plating right at the base of the sealed door is a single, small symbol, freshly scratched into the metal. It's an arrow, pointing away from the bridge and down a side corridor they hadn't yet noticed. Next to the arrow, confirming the message's author beyond any doubt, is another small, quickly drawn symbol: the stylized, open-winged bird sigil of the Altisian Jedi.

With the Force static from the custodian gone he easily pushed his presence beyond the blast doors.   It was empty except for the bright light presence of Echo and another dimmer presence.   His teammate's condition was a welcome relief but the other was a concern.   It wasn’t alive but it also wasn’t a droid or at least one he was familiar with.  He wondered if it was another one like the Custodian that just fought against.  


**”She’s in there,”**  He said urgently as he turned his head towards Roric.  **”She’s awake and determined.  She’s not alone though.   It’s not something alive but it’s, not, a droid.  It’s…I’ve never felt anything like it and it’s focused on a console.”** 


He removed his hand from the blast door and looked around.  As his light scanned the floor he noticed a disturbance in the floor plating.    The dust that permeated everything was disturbed.  In its wake was an arrow etched into the deckplating.  It pointed off down a side corridor he hadn’t noticed until now.  **”Roric!”**  He called out as he knelt down and pointed at the marking that was freshly left.  He looked over and gave Ror a nod.   **”We were left with some bread crumbs.”**  He said as he turned his head to look down the corridor the arrow indicated.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Roric moves to Wes's side, his heavy boots making no sound. He kneels, his own light illuminating the fresh scratches on the deck plating—the arrow and the familiar Altisian sigil. He studies them for a moment, his tactical mind processing the implications.

**"Clever girl,"** he grunts, a rare note of clear admiration in his voice. **"She knew she was being taken. Left us a sign."** He looks down the dark, narrow service corridor the arrow points to. **"A flanking route. She's telling us not to hit the main door. Whatever's in there with her, it's expecting a frontal assault."**

He looks back at Wes, then towards the unconscious form of Sera. **"Alright, Del-Fin. Here's how I see it. We can't leave Nyx here unprotected. And we can't both go down that tunnel. One of us stays, secures Sera, and covers this junction. The other one follows that arrow, finds the back door, and gets Echo out."** His logic is simple, brutal, and efficient. **"It's your command. Who goes, who stays?"**

Wes nodded at Roric’s assessment and choices.  Once again they needed to split.  With Sera remaining unconscious for an unknown amount of time she needed to be looked after.    


**”I’ll go.  You cover and help get Sera up and moving.  Knowing I have you here means I won’t have to worry about something following.   If Sera’s able it will be good to know one or both of you will be backing us up.”**      


He looked at the large blast doors.   **”Comms should be up and working if needed.  If I can open these doors I will.”**  He said looking back at Roric.   **”I’ll be back.”**  He gave Ror a nod and then looked over to Sera.  


He turned and looked down the indicated hallway that Echo pointed out.   Without waiting any longer he turned and started making his way down the hallway.  Somewhere down there was another way into the bridge where Echo and her captor waited.   He moved as quietly as possible with his senses open to any disturbances within the Force.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content: **"Copy that, Wes,"** Roric confirms, his voice a low rumble. **"I'll secure Nyx and hold this position. Move fast. Hit hard. We'll be here."** He turns, his attention already shifting to the tactical problem of securing the junction and protecting their downed teammate, his trust in Wes absolute.

Wes moves into the narrow side corridor. It's a tight, claustrophobic maintenance tunnel, even smaller than the ones they used before, lined with thick, insulated conduits and bundles of data cables. His suit light barely illuminates a few meters ahead, the darkness pressing in. The passage is utterly silent.

He follows the tunnel as it makes a sharp right-angle turn. Ahead, the corridor terminates at a single, vertical maintenance ladder, identical to the one they climbed earlier. It leads up into a dark, square opening in the ceiling. According to the map on his HUD, and Echo's implied direction, this is the way. It's a direct, vertical route to the deck above, where the bridge is located. The path is clear, but the ascent into the unknown darkness is a daunting one.

Reaching the ladder he looked up as he hooked his lightsaber’s O ring to his belt.   He shook his head at the ladder hoping it was in better condition than the last one he was on.   **”I’ve had enough of these maintenance ladders to last a long while.”**  He muttered quietly to the small empty space.    Placing both hands on the rung of the ladder just above his head he pulled down to test its strength.   It didn’t seem to budge.  This area didn’t seem compromised so his guess was that most of the infrastructure should be decently secure to use.   


He looked up and quickly lost sight of the ladder.   It was dark.   He cut the intensity of his lights down to be just enough to see what he needed.  Too much and he’d broadcast to whatever was on the bridge he was coming.   ***I’m coming to you Echo.***  He thought as he put a foot on the first rung and started to climb.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The ladder feels solid under Wes's grip, the ancient metal groaning faintly but holding his weight without the alarming cracks of the previous shaft. He begins his ascent, his dimmed suit light casting a small, moving circle of illumination on the rungs and the tight, dark walls of the maintenance shaft.
