He climbs steadily, the only sounds the soft scrape of his boots on the rungs and the rhythmic hiss of his suit's recycler. He ascends past one deck level, then another, the darkness absolute above and below him. The feeling of isolation is profound.

After climbing roughly twenty meters, he feels a subtle shift in the air—or rather, in the Force. A faint, cold, and unsettling presence, the same "not-alive-but-not-droid" signature he'd sensed from the bridge, begins to bleed into his awareness. He's getting close.

His light catches the top of the shaft just ahead. It opens into a small, grated floor panel in what looks like an avionics or environmental control bay. The panel is slightly ajar, as if it was just recently pushed aside. Peering through the grate, he can see the room is dark, filled with racks of silent, ancient computer systems. On the far side of the room, about ten meters away, is a single, heavy blast door, currently closed. According to his HUD, that door leads directly onto the *Veiled Shadow's* main bridge.

Ascending the ladder was slow and tedious but he needed it to be.  He knew he couldn’t go charging into an unknown situation.   It wasn’t long until he was pushing open the already ajar grate.   


Slowly he opened the grate and stepped up into what looked like a control bay of some sort.   It was dark, empty and quiet.   A door caught his attention on the other side.   It was roughly ten meters away.   He slid the map over on his HUD.   From the looks of it he was in a side chamber.   Past the door was the main bridge.   In there was his scout and teammate and potentially another dangerous droid.   


Moving cautiously he advanced on the door but stopped short of opening it.  Looking at the door for a way to open it he also pushed his presence out beyond the door.  He needed to know where Echo and the droid were when he entered.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Wes approaches the heavy blast door. Physically, it looks unremarkable—a standard, sealed entryway with no obvious external controls on this side, likely operated from the bridge itself.

He closes his eyes, focusing his senses, and pushes his awareness through the thick durasteel. The space beyond feels vast, open, and unnervingly still.

He immediately locks onto Echo's presence. She is about fifteen meters from his position, near the center of the large room. Her mind is a beacon of focused, anxious energy; she is alive, conscious, and intensely concentrated on a task directly in front of her. He can feel the hum of an active console near her.

And right behind her, almost close enough to touch, is the source of the cold, unsettling presence. He gets a sense of its general shape—tall, humanoid, but indistinct and profoundly *wrong*. It is not a droid's familiar emptiness, nor is it the vibrant signature of a living being. It feels like a void that has taken a shape, a concentration of the same disorienting energy from the corridor, now focused into a single, intelligent, and utterly alien point of awareness. Its will is a palpable, crushing weight, directed entirely at Echo and the console she is working on.

Wes felt the two presences beyond the blast door.  One was familiar.   Echo.  She wasn’t calm though.   It was more of a focused control.   Anxious and concentrated on a task.   


The other was vastly different.  Cold and unsettling the presence was close enough to touch his companion.   The familiar unsettling energy he had just fought against felt like it was also just beyond the blast door.  The larger problem was that it was standing guard over Echo.  This presence felt different than the Custodian out in the hallway.  It felt more… dangerous. 


He looked for another way in other than the obvious blast door in front of him.  He didn’t see any and he knew that opening this door was going to be obvious for anyone on the other side.  He took a calming breath and removed his lightsaber from his belt and held it in his left hand.  He looked for the door controls and hit the activation switch.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 1 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Wes scans the wall beside the heavy blast door. Unlike the previous doors, this one has a simple, archaic activation panel, likely because it's an internal maintenance access point. A single, large, illuminated button glows with a soft, steady, pale blue light.

He takes a final, centering breath, his lightsaber hilt held ready in his left hand, and presses the activation button with his right.

With a sudden, explosive hiss of released pressure, the heavy blast door rockets sideways into the wall cavity, its movement far faster and more violent than any of the ancient, grinding doors they'd encountered before. It slams into its recessed housing with a deafening **BOOM** that echoes through the rooms.

The scene on the bridge is revealed in an instant.

The bridge of the *Veiled Shadow* is a vast, cavernous space, its viewports a wall of blackness caked with the grime of ages. Ancient, dark consoles sit in silent, concentric rings. The air is still and dead. Scattered around the bridge are the remains of the *Seventh Moon's* crew, slumped in chairs, their deaths appearing sudden and without struggle.

In the center of the room, bathed in the glow of a single, active console, stands Echo. Her hands are frozen over the controls, her head snapped around towards the sudden, violent noise of the door opening. Her face is pale, her eyes wide with a mixture of shock and a desperate, silent plea.

Standing directly behind her, one shadowy, insubstantial hand resting on her shoulder, is the source of the cold presence. It's a tall, gaunt figure, humanoid in shape but composed of what looks like roiling shadow and flickering static. Its form seems to waver and distort, absorbing the light. It has no discernible features, no face, only a profound sense of ancient, cold intelligence.

As the door slams open, this shadowy entity turns its "head," its featureless face fixing on Wes. The disorienting aura flares out from it, a wave of psychic static and nauseating vertigo that slams into Wes with the force of a physical blow. The entity's other hand rises, pointing a long, shadowy finger at him. A feeling of absolute, soul-chilling dread washes over Wes, a direct, malevolent assault on his mind and spirit.

The blast doors opened far faster and more violently than he had expected.  What had been the norm on the ancient ship was the norm here on the bridge.  Taking that for granted was a mistake he wouldn’t make again.   


With the door now open he could take in the scene of the bridge.   It was like a scene out of a horror movie.  The long dead crew were still at their stations.  It spoke to the fact that perhaps this wasn’t an accident despite the apparent sudden deaths.  The bridge was an ancient tomb untouched by the living for millennia. 


His eyes fell upon echo who was now looking toward him.  He saw the silent plea on her face as he felt her emotions running a gambit.   Upon seeing the figure behind her he now understood why.   


It looked like a specter and instantly he felt the wave of cold and disorientation wash over him.   His posture wavered as he fought to stand tall.   It was similar to the Custodian droid but even this felt different.   Older, deadly.  His hand tightened upon his weapon using it as an anchor. 


He watched the shadowy form as it seemed to float.   He wasn’t sure if it was actually floating as it hadn't moved.   Yet light seemed distorted as it interacted with the shadowy form.  Whatever it was he sensed it was intelligent and ancient perhaps as ancient as the *Shadow* itself.  As the shadow form pointed at him he felt the bone chilling dread hit him.   The assault on his being had begun. 


Summoning the will to move he took a step crossing the threshold onto the bridge.   His training as a Mandalorian and Jedi taking over.  He wasn’t sure where to begin so he started with the basics as his voice sounded out from the  vocoder.   **”I am Wes Del-Fin the Pathfinder.   Release my friend.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The shadowy entity seems to process Wes's words. The crushing psychic pressure lessens for a fraction of a second, a flicker of something akin to curiosity in the ancient, alien mind. But then the pressure returns, cold and absolute. A voice, ancient and dry like dust, echoes not over the comms, but directly in Wes's mind.

***...Pathfinder...*** the thought intrudes, cold and dispassionate. ***...The Key... is not... here. The vessel... must... sleep...***

Wes felt the psychic pressure lessen for just a fraction of a second before it returned to the same cold intensity.  He wondered if what he said had made an impression.   What he got back for an answer confirmed that it did.    


The entity like the Custodian verified that he was the Pathfinder.  What it said next almost shocked him.  That ***The Key*** wasn’t here.  Of course it wasn’t.  It was back on the Chu’unthor as it was found there by the younglings.   What surprised him was its statement that the ship must sleep.   It didn’t make any sense.  Then again the *Shadow* wasn’t a normal ship.   Its ties to the Force weren’t normal. 


He closed his eyes for a second to bring his focus in.   Opening them he looked at the entity.  **”Correct, the key isn’t here but it has been found.  It is safe.  I know where it is.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The shadowy entity processes Wes's declaration. The immense psychic pressure fluctuates, a flicker of what might be surprise or deep contemplation rippling through the Force. The wavering static of its form seems to coalesce, becoming slightly more defined, its featureless "face" tilting as if considering his words.

***...Found...?*** the thought echoes in Wes's mind, laced with an ancient, profound sense of disbelief. ***...The long sleep... can end...?***

The entity's focus seems to shift, its crushing will momentarily lifting from Echo as it turns its full, unnerving attention to Wes. The atmosphere on the bridge changes. The cold, hostile pressure is replaced by an intense, overwhelming wave of ancient hope and yearning. It's a feeling so powerful, so raw after millennia of silent waiting, that it's almost as disorienting as the initial psychic assault.

The entity drifts, its shadowy form gliding silently from behind Echo, moving a few meters closer to Wes.

***...Show... me...*** the thought is a command, a plea, a desperate demand all at once. ***...The Pathfinder... must... guide...***

Wes *heard* the voice in his head again.  It’s disbelief that the Wayfinder had been found was overwhelming.   


**”Yes.  It’s been found.”**  He confirmed and watched as the entity shifted its focus from Echo to himself.  He felt its will turn from Echo towards himself.  He felt the overwhelming hostile emotions take a shift towards overwhelming hope.   The disorienting effect was almost as bad as the hostile emotions.  As the being moved towards him he stayed still.  He simply bent his wrist up and made a small downward motion for Echo to stay put.  He then heard in his mind the spectre's request.   It wanted to see the key.  


He wasn’t sure exactly what to do but he stood steady as he tentatively reached out to the specter with the Force.  He brought up his memory of interacting with the Wayfinder and why it had said to him.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
As Wes reaches out, projecting the memory and the pure concept of the Wayfinder through the Force, the shadowy entity reacts. It stops its advance, hovering silently in the center of the bridge.

The overwhelming wave of hope and yearning intensifies, washing over Wes. The entity is like a being starved for millennia, suddenly presented with the promise of sustenance. It focuses on the memory Wes is projecting, and a new series of impressions, clearer and more urgent than ever before, floods Wes's mind.

He sees a vision, not from the ship, but from the entity itself: a star chart, vast and ancient, but this one is *dark*. The familiar pathways are gone, broken, like shattered threads. He feels an overwhelming sense of *loss*, of a great network of pathways having gone silent and cold. He feels the entity's singular, driving purpose: to *reconnect*, to *reawaken* these dormant paths, a task for which the Wayfinder—the Key—is essential.

And then he feels the entity's demand, a direct query into his own mind, a search for the Key's location.

***...Where...? Where... is... the... Key...? The Path... must be... restored...***

The question is not a simple request; it is a powerful, psychic probe, attempting to pull the information directly from his thoughts. The entity is not hostile, but its ancient, desperate need has no concept of personal boundaries or privacy.

Wes felt hope from the specter when he shared his memory of the Wayfinder or Key as the being in front of him had called it.  When the image of the artifact passed between them he felt a shift in the being.  Hope.  


As more impressions flooded his senses as well as a vision.   This time though it was not from the ship it was from the being.   It was a star chart and Wes recognized it straight away.  It was the same one the Wayfinder showed him but this one was even more incomplete.   What accompanied the vision was another feeling.   An overwhelming sense of loss.   As if something great in scale and hope was dismantled, discarded and lost.  


What he felt next was a need to reconnect and the need for the Wayfinder.  He wasn’t quite ready for what happened next.  The entity’s direct question into his mind.   As if it was searching for the answer and Wes felt the power within the question almost searching for and taking the information.   


Wes turned his head ever so slightly as his head tilted.   He could feel the entity’s need for an answer.   It was an answer that he wasn't ready to give.   He focused his will inward to shore up his defenses.   **”The Key is safe.”**  He paused for a second taking a steadying breath as he could feel the will and need of the entity.   He decided to ask it a question.  **”What is the Path and its purpose?”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
As Wes calmly asserts that the Key is safe, he simultaneously focuses his will, erecting a mental shield to guard the specific knowledge of the *Chu'unthor's* location. He feels the entity's powerful, desperate probe press against his defenses. For a moment, it's an intense struggle, a silent battle of wills in the center of the ancient bridge. His shield holds, but it takes a significant effort of concentration to keep the entity's overwhelming need at bay. The specific location of the Wayfinder remains his own.

The entity seems to recognize the resistance. The psychic pressure of its query recedes slightly, not in defeat, but in a shift of tactics. It has the information it needs most: the Key is not a lost legend; it has been *found*.

When Wes asks his own question—*"What is the Path and its purpose?"*—the entity responds. The cold, dispassionate tone from before is gone, replaced by a resonant, ancient purpose that echoes directly in Wes's mind.

***...The Path... is the... way between... The silent spaces... The hidden currents...***

Another wave of impressions washes over him, less a vision and more a pure, conceptual understanding. He gets a sense of hyperspace, but not as he knows it. This "Path" is a different network, a series of natural, stable corridors through the cosmos, intrinsically linked to the Force itself, separate from the charted hyperspace lanes of the Republic. They are secret, safe, and silent.

***...The purpose... is to... travel... to know... to connect...***

The feeling is one of exploration, of scholarship, of a community of Force-users whose focus was not on galactic politics or warfare, but on traversing these hidden ways, on understanding the deep, quiet mysteries of the galaxy.

***...The Path... is broken. The Wayfinders... are silent. The connection... is lost. The Pathfinder... restores the connection... awakens the Path...***

The entity drifts a fraction closer, the overwhelming hope and yearning returning. It has answered his question. Now, its need is paramount.

***...The Pathfinder... will bring... the Key...***

It is not a question. It is a statement of absolute, unwavering expectation.

Wes, steadfast in his determination to keep the location of the Chu’unthor hidden, did all that he could to keep the entity out of his mind.  It was a tense moment that passed between them until he finally felt the psychic pressure wane slightly.  Again Wes heard the ghostly voice of the entity in his mind.    


It confirmed what Master Davion had told him of the mysteries and loose bits of data the Jedi Master knew.   It was a way to travel outside of the known space lanes.  The implications of that knowledge was unfathomable.   With the current state of the galaxy and the rise of the Empire, a way to travel that didn’t involve any known space lanes was a freedom no one currently had.  


As visions of stable paths and corridors through space flashed in his consciousness the importance of this concept hit home.  Safety for Chu’unthor.  


More feelings poured in from the entity.  Community, learning, a deeper understanding of the Force and secrets of the galaxy.  With all that he was finally beginning to fathom the importance of this discovery.   Yet there was still a question that had been eating at him since the discovery of the Wayfinder.  How and why was he tied to it?


Seeing the specter move slightly closer brought Wes out from his thoughts.   Its question, which sounded more like a demand, fluttered into his mind.   He would bring the key.   


He took a deep breath at the demand.   He had more questions but he wasn’t sure he’d get any answers until he answered the one just posed to him.   He was the expedition's lead but did he have authority over the council to authorize what was demanded.   The council wasn’t here.  He was.  He’d have to make them listen and understand.  If that didn’t work he’d have to make things up on the fly.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: He nodded slowly as he methodically clipped the O ring of his lightsaber to his utility belt.   **”The Key will be brought back but I and my team will need to leave unharmed and unobstructed.  What we came for is needed for our survival.”**  He paused for a second to let his words linger.  **”I also have more questions.   Questions that need answers.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The shadowy entity seems to consider Wes's terms, its form wavering slightly in the dim light of the bridge. The overwhelming psychic pressure of its *need* lessens, replaced by a cold, ancient, and unnervingly logical focus. It has waited for millennia; it can wait for a negotiation.

***...Your... survival... ensures the Key's return...*** the thought echoes in Wes's mind, a statement of cold, pragmatic agreement. ***...The... salvage... is... inconsequential. The dormant... guardians... will remain... dormant. You will... not be... obstructed...***

The entity drifts back a step, a silent concession. Its attention then turns to Echo, who has remained frozen at the console, caught in the psychic crossfire. The intense pressure that had been holding her in place vanishes.

Echo gasps, stumbling back from the console as if released from an invisible grip. She clutches her head for a moment, her breathing ragged over the comms. **"Wes... what... what was that?"** she rasps, her eyes wide with a mixture of fear and awe. **"It was... in my head. Forcing me to... to try and reactivate the long-range comms array. To send a signal..."** She looks from the shadowy entity to Wes, a dawning understanding on her face. **"You... you were talking to it."**

The entity's featureless face turns back to Wes, ignoring Echo now that her purpose is concluded.

***...Ask...*** The single word is a flat, emotionless invitation, a grant of his final term. The entity will answer his questions.

As he felt the psychic pressure of the entity lessen Wes took a deep breath.   He felt that they had hopefully come to an agreement.  He waited looking at the long dead person.  Trying to see anything that may shed some light on who it was.  His wait was short as he heard its words once again in his head.   


Wes gave the entity a nod of his helmet in thanks.  This was one battle he really didn’t want.   He was thankful that words had won out over violence.   He watched as the form drifted backwards a step, looked over at Echo and  ‘released’ her from her confinement.  A mixture of looks and emotions hit him solidly as she fought to make sense of what was happening.  This was clearly outside of her wheelhouse and if he had to be honest with himself, outside his as well.  


**”Yes it needs something.  It will allow us to leave with what we need.   Collect what’s needed here on the bridge.   Contact Roric.   He and Sera are outside the main blast door.   Tell him we’re under condition green.   We won’t be obstructed by the guardians anymore.  I’ll fill you in later.”**  His voice was calm and held that tone of command he had learned from his father.  


He looked back at the floating entity as it stated he could ask more questions.  There were so many but he decided to go with the most important.  He spoke aloud so Echo could hear. **”Who were you, did you have a mission and what happened?   This ship and its droids feel alive or attuned with the Force, how.   Something catastrophic must have occurred.   Crew gone, floating here in the Karthon Expanse.”**  He paused for a second and asked maybe his most important question, at least to him.   **”Why am I the Pathfinder?”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: **"Copy, Wes. Condition green,"** Echo replies, her voice still a little shaky but regaining its professional edge. She quickly moves to a nearby console, her training taking over as she initiates a secure, short-range comm link to Roric's signal just outside the blast doors, relaying Wes's status update. She keeps a wary eye on the shadowy entity, giving it a wide berth.

The entity remains still, a column of shifting shadow in the center of the ancient bridge. As Wes asks his questions, a new series of impressions, more direct and narrative than before, flows into his mind. It is not a conversation, but an offering of pure memory, the final testament of the being that was once the Pathfinder of this vessel.

He feels a sense of *urgency*, of *escape*. The entity was not a Jedi, but a member of a forgotten order of Force-sensitive Navigators. Their mission: to chart the "Path," those hidden, Force-attuned hyperspace routes. The *Veiled Shadow* was their vessel, their archive, their temple.

He gets a flash of a cataclysmic event—not a battle, but a *mishap*. A newly charted "Path" collapsed. An unknown, chaotic Force energy, the "whisper" from the logs, flooded their ship, corrupting their systems, driving their droids mad with its alien logic. The crew, one by one, succumbed to its psychic assault, their minds simply... shutting down.

The final impression is of the Pathfinder on the dais in the power converter annex. Wes feels the being's final, desperate act: a sacrifice. The Pathfinder poured his own life force, his very essence, into the ship's systems, using the lightsaber-key and the colossal crystal to create a paradox—a state of perfect, deep dormancy to contain the chaotic energy, to force the corrupted ship to "sleep" until someone with a compatible resonance could arrive. The skeletal remains are just a shell; the entity before Wes *is* that Pathfinder, his consciousness now a non-corporeal echo bound to the ship he saved.

***...Why... you...?*** The entity seems to search for an answer, and the impression that returns is simple, a fundamental truth it perceives in Wes. ***...The blood... remembers the Path. The spirit... seeks the way. You... are... of the line...***

Wes felt the influx of actual memories from the entity.  It was then that he realized what or more importantly who the shadowy entity was.   It was the *Shadows* own pathfinder.  They weren’t Jedi but a forgotten order. Mapping and exploring hidden Force attuned hyperspace routes.  This ship was their home.  


More memories came to him and they were shocking.   The take over of the ship from a Force entity.  It corrupted ship systems and droids alike.  The psychic assault on the crew that caused their minds to simply shut down sent a shiver down his back.    


The scene changed and he recognized the main engineering room that the team was in earlier.  The raised dias and the solitary figure that remained.   Wes remembered the lightsaber the form held along with the massive crystal that ebbed and flowed with Force energy.  He effectively put the ship to sleep until the time came that someone could help.  He gave his own life in the hope they would eventually be rescued.   

The words that came next almost made him step back.   That he was *of the line* of pathfinders.   He shook his head in disbelief.  He almost asks how that was possible but didn’t.    


More questions sprang to mind as he again spoke aloud. **”If the *Shadow* is dormant to stop the corrupting Force energy won’t releasing the ship from its dormancy cause the same chain of events to start again?  How does the key factor into it”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The shadowy form of the Pathfinder's echo remains still, its presence a cold, ancient weight in the silent bridge. It processes Wes's logical, cautious question. The response that flows back into his mind is a mixture of ancient knowledge and a profound, lingering warning.

***...The... corruption... sleeps... but is not... dead. Awakening the vessel... risks... awakening *it*...***

A new impression forms, a vision of pure mechanics and Force energy. Wes sees the Wayfinder, the Key, not just as a map, but as an *instrument of control*.

***...The Key... is not... just for guidance. It is... for attunement. It resonates... with the Heart...*** He gets a powerful impression of the colossal crystal in engineering, identifying it as the "Heart" of the ship. ***...The Pathfinder... uses the Key... to harmonize the Heart... to purge the whispers... to calm the storm...***

The final impression is one of immense, daunting responsibility.

***...Without the Key... awakening the vessel... is... madness. The storm... will consume all. With the Key... the Path... can be made... safe again...***

The shadowy voice of the Pathfinder echoed in his head once more.  It confirmed his suspicion of reactivating the ship.  The chaotic Force energy or entity would most likely awaken.   Then a new impression came to him.   The Wayfinder wasn’t just a map.  It was *the* Instrument of control.


The ghostly Pathfinder's use of the word key initially confused him.   He initially thought of the Wayfinder as a map.   It simply got them from point A to point B.   Wes wasn't thinking on a big enough scale.  The Wayfinder was *The* key to making the ship work. It was attuned to the *Heart* as the Pathfinder called it.  Wes, once again, saw the image of the giant crystal in the engineering bay.  He nodded as he understood.   The Key and the Heart together would purge the whispers from the *Shadow.*


It was painfully clear that the Wayfinder needed to be brought to the *Shadow.* Again he spoke aloud.   **”I have so many questions.   How do I use the Key?    Are there other ships and Pathfinders?  If there are, how do I find them?  Is the entire Path compromised?”**   The questions came quickly as he didn’t know how much time he had left.  The enormity of this discovery was only just beginning to settle.   If the Empire found this ship the consequences were too vast to comprehend.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Wes's questions, urgent and vital, pour into the silent bridge, directed at the shadowy echo of the Pathfinder. He feels the entity's ancient consciousness consider them, a swirl of immense, fragmented knowledge.

It attempts to answer. He feels a surge of information, a chaotic torrent of impressions: glimpses of other, similar ships adrift in silent voids, images of star charts so vast they defied comprehension, the faint, sorrowful echoes of other Pathfinders, long silent, long lost. He feels the concept of "using the Key"—an act of will, of Force attunement, of a ritual he doesn't understand.

But the effort is too much. The entity's form, a fragile construct of memory and Force energy held together by a singular, millennia-old purpose, begins to fray. The connection, strained by this direct and complex communication, starts to collapse.

***...The... Path... is... broken...*** the thought comes, weaker now, laced with a profound, final weariness. ***...The... Key... must... return... Find... the... others... Awaken... the...***

The thought cuts off. The shadowy figure wavers violently, the static of its form intensifying. The pale blue light that defines it flickers, dims, and then, with a final, silent implosion, it vanishes completely.

The oppressive, intelligent presence that had filled the bridge is gone. The psychic pressure vanishes, leaving behind only the deep, neutral, dormant resonance of the ship itself. The whispers are silent. The bridge is once again just an ancient, dusty tomb.

Wes listened as the ancient Pathfinder spoke.   Its thought and words brought forth images of other ships and star charts that he couldn’t even begin to grasp.  The impression of “using the key” was something he didn’t hold a concept of or understand. 


He felt the shift in the Force.  In the Pathfinders presence.   It was weakening and Wes, desperate for more answers, took a step forward.  He stopped when it spoke for what would be the last time.  The projected thoughts felt tired.  There was no other way to explain it.    **”The path is broken.  The key must return. Find the others. Awaken the…”**  He said the words softly as the ancient Pathfinder said them.    


He waited for more but none came.  He watched the light diminish from the shadowy form as it became unstable and then imploded upon itself and winked out of existence.  


**”Kriff!”** Wes exclaimed.   The sudden exclamation had caused Echo to turn her head from the station she was at.   A delicate and needed part in her right hand.  


He shook his head and gave her an “all clear” signal.   **”It's gone.  I think maybe for good.”  His voice held a bit of regret.  There was much he still needed to know.**”  


He opened up his comms to the team. Hoping that maybe the make shift relay would send it to the entire team.  **”Roric, we're clear here.  I'm going to see if I can open the blast doors.   How’s Sera?”**  As he waited for an answer he moved to a station and attempted to find the blast door controls.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: Echo gives a final, steadying nod, securing the small, crystalline Nav-Matrix Stabilizer she'd carefully extracted from the console into a padded pouch on her utility belt. **"Objective secured,"** she reports, her voice regaining more of its usual calm professionalism. She turns from the console, her gaze sweeping the now-silent bridge. The absence of the oppressive, shadowy entity is a palpable relief. **"I'm alright, Wes. A little rattled, but functional. Whatever that thing was, its hold is gone."** She moves towards the main bridge entrance, ready to assist.
Roric's gruff voice crackles over the comms, a welcome sound of normalcy. **"Copy, Wes. We're clear out here. Nyx is starting to come around. Still groggy, but she's responding."**

As Wes approaches the main bridge control station, the one Echo had been working at, he finds the console still glowing with a faint, residual light. The archaic symbols are still displayed, but they seem less active now. With a bit of searching, he finds a panel with a series of heavy, physical switches. One is clearly labeled with a symbol that matches the blast door's design.

He throws the switch. With a deep, groaning **CLUNK** and a hiss of ancient pneumatics, the massive blast door to the main corridor slides open, revealing Roric standing guard over the slowly stirring form of Sera. The way is clear.

Wes nodded at Echo.  Her all clear and “ready go” was a welcome response.     He decided to tell her about the Pathfinder.  **”The entity was the ship's Pathfinder.  A navigator that uses the Force to traverse hidden hyperspace lanes that are linked to the Force.”**  He saw her raise an eyebrow and he smiled.   **”I know it sounds like a fairytale.”**   He looked at the console as he ran his hands over it.   


Finding what he wanted he moved the lever causing a loud groan and clunk from the ancient blast door.   Outside he was happy to see Roric and a slow moving Sera still sitting on the floor. Looking up he gave Ror a thumbs up.   **”Alright we have what we came for.”**  He looked around and adding **”And then some.  Let’s get the Kriff out of here.”**  


He looked around the bridge once more.   The dead crew were still at their stations.  He looked to where the *Shadows* Pathfinder has disappeared.   **”I will be back, with the key.”**  


He walked out the newly opened blasé doors and looked at Sera.   He gave her a nod and offered her a hand up.  **”Welcome back?”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: Sera groans softly, her gloved hand going to her helmeted head. She looks up, her eyes still unfocused as she takes Wes's offered hand. **"Ugh... thanks,"** she mutters, allowing him to help her to her feet. She's unsteady for a moment, leaning against the bulkhead. **"Felt like my brain was in a static storm. What... what happened? I remember that weird droid, the fight... and then just... noise."**

Roric, seeing that Sera is conscious and the way is clear, gives a curt nod. **"Good to have you back, Nyx. Let's move. We've spent enough time in this tomb."** He falls into his familiar rear-guard position, his rifle scanning the dark corridor behind them as the team finally begins its exfiltration from the bridge.

The journey back down through the maintenance shafts and service corridors is tense but blessedly uneventful. The aura field is gone, the dormant droids remain dormant, and the ship is as silent as it was when they first arrived. The path is now familiar, and they make good time, their shared sense of urgency a palpable presence in the quiet of their comms.

Finally, they emerge from the service tunnel back into the vast, hidden hangar bay. In the distance, the *Seventh Moon* sits, a silent, dark shape. And beside it, the massive, hovering forms of the repulsor sleds, laden with the colossal power converters, wait patiently.

Wes nodded and made sure Sera had her footing before letting go of her hand.   She found the bulkhead to be solid and used it for a moment to reorient herself.  Her question about what happened had him looking at each of them.  **”I’m still in a bit of shock and disbelief.  The entity that was on the bridge was the ship’s Pathfinder.  It’s Navigator.  It used the Force to travel hidden hyperspace routes throughout the galaxy.  Perhaps farther than we could ever know.  This ship needs the key.  The key is needed to purge the *Shadow* of the invading Force energy.   That’s why the ship is in stasis to prevent the chaotic energy from taking over.   The ship's Pathfinder gave his life to put the *Shadow* into stasis.”** He shook his head and let out a breath.  Let’s head back.  **”The *Shadows* droids should all be standing down now.  The Pathfinder saw to it before it left or became one with the Force.”**   


Wes was thankful when he saw the corridor that let to the secret hanger where the *Seventh Moon* was waiting for them.  It looked like a giant guardian waiting.  The repulsor sled was next to it still carrying the massive power converters they salvaged earlier.   They just needed to be loaded.    


Not seeing Marina Wes opened the teams comms.  **”Marina, we're back.  What’s the situation here?”**  He reached out with the Force.   Pushing his awareness out to encompass the hanger bay.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: Marina's voice responds almost immediately, a beacon of calm professionalism in the silent hangar. **"Copy that, Wes. Welcome back. Good to hear your voices."**

As Wes extends his senses, he feels her presence inside the *Seventh Moon*, in the engine room. She's not idle; he can feel the focused, methodical energy of her work, a calm hum of concentration amidst the dormant machinery. The repulsor sled with the power converters is positioned perfectly near the Ghtroc's main cargo hatch, just as they'd left it.

**"Situation here is stable,"** her voice continues over the comm. **"I've finished the preliminary diagnostics on the *Moon's* main power core and repulsorlift systems. I've mapped out the power-up sequence and prepped the bypasses for the ignition relays. We're ready to start the main engine cycle on your command. The converters are ready to be loaded. Just waiting on you."** A brief pause, then a more personal note enters her tone. **"Everything go alright up there?"**

He felt her presence in the aft section.   Her focused determination was directed and purposeful.   Hearing Marina’s voice brought a smile to his face.    He led the contingent onto the main deck towards the *Moon* as she spoke.   From her report it sounded like she had accomplished much in the time they were gone.  Her question about how things went was answered after a short pause.  


**”Successful but not without difficulties and an Illuminating encounter with the *Shadows* own Pathfinder.   We have the Nav-Matrix Stabilizer in hand.  We’ll be aboard in a moment.  There’s a lot to fill in once we’re away.  We will be aboard in a moment.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
As Wes and his team approach the *Seventh Moon*, the Ghtroc freighter sits dark and silent, a ghost within a ghost ship. The auxiliary cargo hatch they'd used earlier remains open to the vacuum of the hangar.

Wes leads the way, stepping from the dusty deck of the *Veiled Shadow* into the equally silent and airless cargo hold of the *Seventh Moon*.

As they move through the hold into the connecting passage, Marina's voice comes over the comms again, this time from much closer, likely from an internal speaker just beyond the next bulkhead. **"Wes, hold up a second. I'm cycling the inner hold airlock now."**

A heavy interior bulkhead door, one Wes might not have paid much attention to on his first pass-through, seals shut with a pneumatic hiss. A series of indicator lights on the door, previously dark, flicker to life. For a moment, there is silence, then a new sound—the rush of air—is faintly audible through the deck plating and their helmets. After about a minute, a light on the door panel switches from red to a steady green. The inner door slides open, revealing Marina standing in a dimly lit but now fully pressurized corridor. She has her helmet off, her face showing a mixture of fatigue and professional pride.

**"Life support is active, but only on the ship's internal sections for now,"** she reports, her voice now clear and direct, without the filter of the comm system. **"The cargo hold is still in vacuum. I figured it'd be easier to load the converters that way. Once they're aboard and we seal the outer hatch, we can pressurize th hold as well."**

As the airlock cycled, Wes smiled.  Looking around he saw that the door control lights were lit and flashing.  The sound of rushing air was a relief and it meant that Marina had been very active in the time they were gone.  When the airlock was pressurized the inner door hissed open.   


Waiting for them was Marina.  The first thing he saw was a small proud smile at the work she had gotten done.  The second was more felt than seen.  She was tired.   **”You’ve been busy.”**  He walked up closer and removed his helmet.   It was dirty like the rest of his armor that also sported several new battle scars.  **”I’ll help get the cargo loaded and then we can help with the ship.”**  He looked over to Sera.   **”See if you get a message to Kael.  Check in with him and let him know we got the rest of the salvage we needed.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: He stepped closer to Marina as reached out to her with the Force.  **”How are you two feeling?”** He said with a lower voice.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: Sera nods to Wes, finding a spot near the cockpit's comms station. **"Trying now,"** she says, tapping into the newly powered console. **"I'm pushing the relay signal Marina set up. Bouncing it off the hangar bay walls to try and get a clear line of sight... but the *Shadow's* hull is just too dense. It's like trying to shout through a mountain. I'm getting nothing back but static. Kael won't hear us from in here."**

Marina returns Wes's smile as he removes his helmet, but her expression quickly shifts to one of concern as her eyes trace the new, angry-looking scorch marks on his beskar armor. She doesn't ask, not over the open comms, but her hand comes up, her fingers gently brushing the edge of a fresh scar on his chest plate, her touch a silent question.

When he speaks to her in a lower voice, her gaze softens. **"Tired,"** she admits quietly, leaning into his Force-presence for a moment. **"But we're okay. Both of us."** Her hand drops from his armor, her focus returning to the mission. **"With Sera's news, it looks like one of us is going to have to make the trip back to the *Skimmer* on foot. We can't leave Kael in the dark."**

Wes felt her concern when she noticed the new battle scar on the chest plate.  He watched as her fingers gently touched the newly blackened scar that marked his armor.   He gave her a nod as he let her know through the bond they shared that he was alright.   **”I’m alright.  Everyone was okay.  It’s good that we went up there.  I’ll tell you more on the way back.”**   


Sera’s news on not being able to reach Kael brought a tight lipped grimace to his face as Marina voiced the same concern he was feeling.  **”Alright,”**  He scanned his team.  **”We need to get word to him and get the cargo loaded.  Marina,”**  Wes turned back to her.   **”Estimation on a time for the *Moon* to be fired up and ready to go?  We don’t have to worry about waking the droids now so bring up systems as you need to.”**


He looked over to Sera and Echo.  **”Keep trying to get a signal out, maybe we’ll get lucky.  When the cargo is loaded and secure you two will go rendezvous with Kael.  Before you two go we will get an update from Marina on a time frame.”**  He looked at everyone and saw them nodding.  **”Alright.  Roric with me let’s get the cargo loaded and secure.  Echo, Sera work with Marina.   Let’s get this bucket ready and go home.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: **"Copy, Wes,"** Echo confirms with a sharp nod. **"Sera and I will make the run back to the *Skimmer* as soon as the cargo is secure here. We'll give Kael the full sit-rep."**

**"I'll keep trying to punch a signal through,"** Sera agrees, though her expression suggests she's not optimistic. **"But yeah, a direct courier run is our most reliable option. We'll be ready to move when you are."**
Roric simply grunts his acknowledgment and moves towards the open cargo hatch, ready to assist Wes with the heavy lifting, his focus already on the immediate, physical task.

Marina turns her attention to the cockpit's engineering console, which is now glowing with auxiliary power. **"Alright, Wes,"** she begins, her voice taking on the focused, analytical tone of a master mechanic. **"To go from this state—auxiliary power online—to a full, stable flight-ready status... it's a multi-stage process."**

She brings up a complex power-up sequence on the main display. **"First, I'll need to use the auxiliary power to pre-charge the main capacitors. That'll take about twenty minutes. Once they're charged, we can initiate the primary fusion cycle in the Cryo-Cycle core. That's the 'one-shot' moment I mentioned. It'll be a massive energy spike, and from that point, we'll be on a very loud, very visible clock."**

She continues, tracing the flowcharts. **"Assuming the core ignites cleanly, it'll take another twenty to thirty minutes to bring the main sublight engines and repulsorlifts to a stable, operational temperature and flush the coolant lines. Then another ten minutes for final system checks and to power up the nav-computer for the jump calculations Kael will need to make once we're clear."**

She looks up at him, her expression serious. **"Best case scenario, from the moment we decide to start the main power-up sequence, we're looking at a minimum of **fifty minutes to an hour** before this ship can actually lift off the deck and fly out of here. And that's assuming there are no unexpected... complications... with a 75-year-old engine that's been in a deep freeze."**

Wes gave Echo and Sera a nod as they both prepped for the next stage.    Looking over to Roric who was already by the cargo hatch he smiled,    The veteran soldier was already ready to get to work.   **”I’ll be ready in a moment.”**  He said as he turned to Marina.  He could sense she had an update on the *Moon*


He took a breath of the cycling air and looked at her.  **”How’s she looking?”** He asked of the *Moon.*  He smiled when she started the run down.  He was always amazed at the change in focus when she got down to what she was very good at.  


It was obvious she had a concrete plan on the start up for the *Moon*.  There were some ‘ifs’ thrown in but the plan was there.   Fifty to sixty minutes from  startup to flying out of the landing bay.   Providing that all goes according to plan.   **”Fifty to Sixty minutes.”**  He gave her a nod and smiled.  **”Good work.”**  He said as he looked over to where Roric waited.  **”Ror and I will get the converters loaded up and secure.   Have Echo give out a hand if needed.  Let’s try to coordinate when they get back to Kael and get ready for our own departure.  We’ll set up a rendezvous point nearby in case either or has any issues getting off this bucket.”**  He paused for a second as another thought came to mind.   **”We do have a working way out of this hanger bay don’t we?”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Marina nods, her mind already running through the sequence. **"The main hangar door... it's still sealed. The control panel is unpowered,"** she confirms, pulling up the schematic of the hidden bay. **"But the good news is, it's tied into the same local power grid as the turbolift. Once we start the main power-up sequence on the *Seventh Moon*, I can divert a fraction of that power to the hangar controls. It should be enough to cycle the main door open for our departure."**


She considers his point about coordination. **"A rendezvous point is a smart idea. Once Echo and Sera get back to the *Skimmer*, have Kael transmit the coordinates for a low-orbit rally point, somewhere just outside this asteroid field. He can use a tight-beam burst that the *Moon's* passive sensors should be able to pick up, even if we can't reply. That way, we'll know where to meet them once we're clear of the *Shadow*."**

She gives him a final, confident look. **"Alright. I'll get the capacitor pre-charge started. It's the quietest part of the process. You and Roric get those converters loaded. Let's make this happen."**

**”Good.  After the converters are loaded up I’ll set up the rendezvous point with the two of them and with the power diversion for the main bay doors we should be set.  If not, we have other means.”**


With a plan and assignments set he gave her a nod. **”We shouldn’t be too long.  We'll make sure they are locked down tight.”**  He gave her a nod a second before bringing up his helmet and securing it back into place.  He brought up his armor's environmental systems on his HUD and made sure they were running.  Turning he motioned to Roric.   **”Let's get them loaded.   Get the ship's cargo bay open I’ll head outside.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: Wes secures his helmet, the familiar hiss of its environmental seals engaging. He gives Roric a nod, and the two armored figures move through the make shift airlock towards the open cargo hatch of the *Seventh Moon*.

Roric steps into the airless hold and heads for the main cargo ramp controls. With a low groan of ancient machinery, the Ghtroc's primary ramp begins to lower, its heavy form descending to meet the dusty deck of the hidden hangar.

Outside, Wes expertly pilots the repulsor sled, laden with the two colossal power converters, maneuvering it carefully towards the newly opened ramp. The sled's repulsorlifts hum as they strain under the immense weight.

Loading the converters is a slow, painstaking process. Wes, from the sled's controls, and Roric, acting as a spotter and guide on the ramp, work in tandem. They use the sled's localized repulsorlift field to carefully levitate the multi-ton converters, inch by inch, up the ramp and into the Ghtroc's cargo hold. The process is a delicate ballet of precise controls and gruff, one-word hand signals from Roric, ensuring the massive components don't scrape the sides of the entryway or topple from the sled.

After a tense twenty minutes, both High-Yield Power Converters are safely inside the *Seventh Moon's* cargo hold.
**"Cargo's in,"** Roric grunts over the comms, already moving to secure the massive components to the deck with heavy-duty magnetic clamps. **"They're not going anywhere."**
Just as Roric finishes, Echo's voice cuts in over the team channel. **"Wes, we're ready to move on your mark. Standing by at the service corridor entrance."**

With the massive power converters now secure in the *Moon’s* cargo bay Wes gave Roric a nod as he watched the veteran move to lock them down.   The tension was rising as they were moving closer to leaving the *Shadow*.   The cargo was secure.   New knowledge had been found out and they now had a new ship on which half the team and precious cargo were going to rely on to get home.  He was thankful Marina was here to make sure all could go right.   


As Echo reported over the team's channel, he looked over to the service corridor entrance.  He gave the pair a wave.  **”Copy Echo.  Hold tight.”** He paused for just a second. **”Marina what’s the status?  Are we good to give  Echo and Sera to go ahead to rendezvous with Kael and begin the *Moon’s*  up process?”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Marina's voice comes back over the comm, calm and focused, from the Ghtroc's cockpit. **"I've prepped everything I can on auxiliary power, Wes. The pre-charge sequence for the main capacitors is programmed and ready to go. All other pre-flight checks are green on my board."**

She pauses, the weight of the next, irreversible step evident in her tone. **"I'm ready to begin the main power-up sequence on your command. Once I start, we're committed. We'll be on that one-hour clock, and the *Veiled Shadow* will know we're here. If Team Skimmer is leaving, now is the time to send them."**

**”Copy Marina.  Go ahead and start the process in five minutes.  As far as waking up the ship we should be ok.  The Pathfinder said the dormant guardians will remain dormant.  That we will not be obstructed.”**  He paused for a quick second.  **”As a precaution though let’s be vigilant.  Let’s keep an eye out.  Roric and I will take up defensive positions.   As soon as you need me in the cockpit let me know.”**  He took a quick breath.  **”Echo, Sera make for Kael’s position.  We should be out of here in fifty-five to sixty five minutes.  One sec before you go.”**  He brought up the star chart of the area surrounding the *Shadow.*    **”Sending you both coordinates for a rendezvous point nearby.”**  A few seconds passed and he sent the coordinates off.  **”Coordinates have been sent.  Be safe.  We’ll see the three of you in about an hour.  May the Force be with you.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: **"Coordinates received, Wes,"** Echo's voice confirms, a crisp, professional acknowledgment. **"We'll see you at the rally point. Be careful in here. Echo out."**

Without another word, the two suit lights belonging to Echo and Sera turn in unison and disappear back into the dark maw of the service corridor. Their footsteps make no sound in the vacuum as they begin their long, lonely trek back to the *Void Skimmer*. A palpable sense of finality, of a crew now divided by a vast and dangerous distance, settles over the hidden hangar.

Roric grunts his acknowledgment and moves with a soldier's economy of motion to a new defensive position near the Ghtroc's open ramp. He kneels, bracing his ion rifle on a piece of nearby wreckage, his field of fire covering the main entrance to the hangar bay. His posture becomes one of absolute, unwavering vigilance.

In the cockpit of the *Seventh Moon*, Marina's voice is a steady presence over the comm. **"Copy, Wes. Five-minute countdown to pre-charge sequence initiated. I'll call you up to the cockpit when we're ready for the main ignition cycle. The board is green for now."**

Wes takes his own position near the ramp, his own weapon held at a low ready, his senses extended. He feels for any shift in the *Veiled Shadow's* profound Force-presence, any hint of a reaction to their plan. There is nothing but the deep, ancient, and now neutral-feeling hum.

The five-minute timer on his HUD ticks down with agonizing slowness. The silence in the hangar is absolute, broken only by the soft hiss of their suit recyclers. 5:00... 4:00... 3:00...

At the two-minute mark, Marina's voice returns.
**"Pre-charge sequence commencing in two minutes. All systems are go. Wes... get ready."**

The final seconds tick away. At 0:00, a low, deep **HUMMMMM** begins to emanate from the *Seventh Moon*, a sound that vibrates through the deck plating. The previously dark running lights along the Ghtroc's hull flicker to life with a weak, hesitant amber glow. Inside, the main capacitors are beginning to draw power, the first step in waking the ancient ship from its long slumber. The hangar remains silent. The droids do not stir. The Pathfinder's promise, for now, holds true.

Wes looked over the hanger.  All was quiet yet he felt like he was waiting for the other shoe to drop.   He just hoped that the Pathfinders' promise that the ship's guardians would stay dormant held true.   Starting up the engines was going to be the de facto point of no return.  


Wes watched as the clock ticked down to 0:00 and right on queue he felt the deep hum and vibration through the deck plating.   **”Here we go.”**  He said as he expanded his senses out in an attempt to sense any awakening.   


He felt nothing.  The hanger was silent except for the awakening freighter.


**”Marina,”** He said calmly over the team's channel.  **”I’m not going to jinx it by saying anything other than keep going.”**     


Taking a breath Wes focused inward finding his calm.  As he let his breath out he expanded his senses back out into the hanger and farther outward into the *Shadow*”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The low hum from the *Seventh Moon* intensifies, a steady thrum that speaks of ancient machinery slowly, painstakingly coming to life. Inside the cockpit, Marina monitors the power flow with absolute focus, her hands poised over the console, ready to cut the sequence at the slightest sign of instability. The twenty-minute pre-charge cycle feels like an eternity.

Throughout the process, Wes maintains his vigilant watch, his Force senses stretched taught, probing the silence of the *Veiled Shadow*. He feels nothing but the same deep, dormant resonance. The cold voids of the droids remain cold, their standby status unchanged. The Pathfinder's promise holds.

Finally, Marina's voice, tight with concentration, comes over the comm. **"Pre-charge complete. Main capacitors are at optimal levels for ignition. Wes... this is it. The point of no return. I need you in the cockpit for this. Once I start the fusion cycle, you'll need to be ready to take the helm. I'll have to manage the power distribution and engine diagnostics second by second."**

It was a tense wait yet it was also a quiet wait.  Quiet was good.   When Marina’s voice came across the comms he looked at the chrono.   Time was just about up.  


**”Copy Marina.  On my way.”**  Wes moved from his position and quickly made his way over to Roric.   As he did he removed the Ion grenade from his bandolier.  **”Just in case.  See you inside.”**  With a nod and a heavy pat on Roric’s shoulder he quickly turned and made his way up the gangplank and headed for the cockpit.   Sliding into the pilots chair he buckled in knowing it was going to be a bumpy ride.  


His hands began moving over the controls.  **”I’m at the controls Marina.  Tell me what you need.”**  His eyes looked for the weapon systems controls to see if they had power and could be controlled from the cockpit.  It was still quiet but he wanted to be ready for anything.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: **"Copy, Wes. Acknowledged,"** Marina's voice replies, a low hum of machinery audible in the background of her transmission. **"I'm in engineering. The pre-charge is stable. I'm bypassing the fried ignition relays now. Once I start the main fusion cycle, you'll see the primary power grid light up on your board, starting with the repulsorlifts and sublight engines. I'll need you to monitor the energy flow and keep the system from overloading as it comes online. Be ready."**

The Ghtroc's cockpit is still dim, running only on the auxiliary power Marina had established. Wes's hands move over the controls. The weapon systems console is dark, showing no signs of power. It's clear that only the most essential systems are currently active, and firing up the ship's defenses will require diverting power from the main core once it's online.

A few tense moments pass. Then, a new, deeper **HUMMM** begins to vibrate through the ship's frame, and the cockpit lights flare to full intensity. On the console before Wes, the power grid schematic begins to light up, section by section, a wave of energy flowing from the engineering icon towards the repulsorlift and engine systems. The deep hum vibrating through the Ghtroc's frame intensifies, the ship groaning as its ancient heart begins to beat for the first time in decades.

**"Core ignition is successful!"** Marina's voice crackles over the comm, filled with a mixture of professional triumph and relief. **"I'm getting a clean fusion cycle. Rerouting power to sublight engines and repulsorlifts now. Watch those energy flow regulators, Wes!"

Wes felt the ship shudder as it came to life for the first time in decades.   He was watching the console as it began to light up showing systems coming online.  A smile appeared at Marina’s ability in engineering.  **”Controls are coming online. Repulsor esp and engines are looking green.”**  


Her comment about the energy flow regulators had him looking to the other side of the controls.   **”Copy.    Energy flow is looking good.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: The Ghtroc's ancient systems roar to life around Wes, the cockpit lighting up like a nova as the main power core ignites. His hands fly across the console, his expert touch diverting excess energy from the initial surge, stabilizing the flow, and preventing the very overload Marina had feared. Green lights cascade across the board as the ship's heart beats strong and steady for the first time in three-quarters of a century.

Marina's triumphant voice cuts through the comms from the engine room, layered over the powerful hum of the newly awakened core. **"Core is stable, Wes! We have a clean fusion cycle! Repulsorlifts and sublight engine power are green! We're ahead of schedule—I think we can be ready to lift in thirty minutes, maybe less!"**
From the cargo hold, Roric's voice is a low, steady rumble. **"Cargo's secure. Ramp is ready to cycle on your command, Del-Fin. Standing by to load."**

**”Green across the board here.   Engines and repulsors are looking good.”**  His fingers moved along the board as his feet and body felt the smooth vibration as the ship's engines came fully online.  **”Thirty minutes or less.  Copy that.”**  His fingers danced along the controls keeping any power surges in check.  


He took a quick look outside the cockpit window as Roric checked in.   **”Copy Roric.  Stand by.”**  He let his senses once again expand out.  Searching for any indication from the ship now that they potentially set off every alarm the ship had.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
Wes extends his senses, reaching out from the newly awakened bubble of life that is the *Seventh Moon* into the vast, ancient silence of the *Veiled Shadow*. He braces for a reaction—an angry flare from the dormant droids, a hostile surge from the ship's 'consciousness'—but finds... nothing.

The droids remain cold, inert voids in the Force, their standby status unchanged by the Ghtroc's resurrection. The Pathfinder's promise holds. The immense, ambient Force-resonance of the derelict remains a steady, deep hum, like a sleeping titan completely undisturbed by the flea that has just stirred on its back. The power-up sequence, while a massive event within the hidden hangar, was apparently too localized or too insignificant to register as a threat to the slumbering behemoth.

Nothing.


As his senses pushed out past the secret hanger he sensed nothing amiss.  No hidden stirring of long dormant cleaner droids.  All was quiet.  He slowly let out his breath and relaxed.  The Pathfinders word seemed to be holding true.  The *Shadows* droids were still inert.   **”The *Shadow* is quiet.  The engine start up hasn’t caused any alerts.  The Pathfinders promise has held.”**


His hand moved across the controls tweaking settings as needed.  Making sure readings stayed in the green.  **”Still green across the board here.  Temps are rising steadily.”**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
**"Copy that, Wes,"** Marina's voice replies over the internal comm, her relief palpable. **"That's one less thing to worry about. Down here, the power flow from the core is stable, and I'm getting green lights on all primary repulsorlift conduits. She's ready to lift as soon as we are."**

The minutes tick by in a hum of productive, focused energy. With the converters already loaded, Roric secures the final magnetic clamps, ensuring the multi-ton salvage won't shift an inch during flight. Wes continues to monitor the Ghtroc's systems from the cockpit, his board a cascade of green indicators as the ancient ship's heart beats strong and steady.

Finally, Marina's voice returns, this time from much closer as she enters the cockpit behind him, her helmet now clipped to her belt. **"Alright, all pre-flight checks are complete from engineering. She's as ready as she'll ever be. The cargo ramp is sealed and the hold is pressurized. We're go for launch on your command."**

**”Copy Marina.  Confirmed green across the board.”** Wes said confidently as his eyes scanned the cockpit controls.  The *Moon* was stable and the smooth vibration beneath his feet was testimony to Marina’s technical ability.   **”Roric time for last minute checks.  Make sure all is secure and then get strapped in.”**  


His hands moved quickly over the controls.  He looked over his shoulder when he heard Marina's voice.   He smiled beneath his helmet.  **”Good.”**  He said and indicated the co-pilots chair.   **”Keep an eye on her as I lift off.”**  


Roric entered the cockpit with his usual stoicism and strapped in.   **”Alright.  We’re all here.  How are we on time for the rendezvous with the *Skimmer?*  Are we going to be on time?”**  He said calmly as his hand came to rest on the repulsor controls.   With a slow and steady hand he applied power.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: Marina nods, slipping into the co-pilot's chair and immediately strapping herself in. Her own hands move with practiced familiarity over the secondary console, bringing up the detailed engine and power diagnostics. **"I've got the board, Wes. All systems nominal. Go for lift-off."** She glances at the mission chrono on her display. **"And as for the rendezvous... if we lift now, and accounting for the time it will take Echo and Sera to reach the *Skimmer*, we should arrive at the rally point with about ten, maybe fifteen minutes to spare. It'll be tight, but we're on schedule."**
From the acceleration couch just behind them, Roric's gruff voice confirms his readiness. **"Strapped in and secure back here."**

Wes applies power to the repulsorlifts. With a deep, powerful **HUM** that vibrates through the entire Ghtroc, the ancient freighter lifts from the deck of the hidden hangar, its landing struts retracting with a series of heavy **CLUNKS**. The *Seventh Moon*, dormant for seventy-five years, is airborne once more.

Wes expertly pilots the ship, its controls surprisingly responsive despite their age. He guides the Ghtroc out of its resting place and into the center of the bay.
**"I'm cycling the main hangar door now,"** Marina reports, her fingers flying over her console as she interfaces with the bay's external system.

On the far side of the hidden bay, a massive section of what looked like the outer hull of the *Veiled Shadow* begins to retract with a low, powerful groan. Colossal locking mechanisms disengage, and the bay door slides open, revealing not a corridor, but the star-dusted, debris-strewn blackness of the Graveyard Shallows. The path to open space is clear.

Wes eases the throttle forward, intending a smooth exit from the bay. But as the *Seventh Moon* glides towards the opening, an unexpected gravitic eddy from the surrounding debris field tugs at the freighter. The Ghtroc, its ancient inertial dampeners struggling to compensate after decades of disuse, lurches violently to port.

A deafening **SCREEEEECH** of tortured metal echoes through the cockpit as the freighter's port-side mandible scrapes hard against the edge of the hangar bay doorframe. Red warning lights flash across the console, and the ship shudders from the impact, a jarring, bone-rattling collision. A high-pitched alarm begins to shriek through the cockpit.

**"Hull breach, port mandible!"** Marina shouts, her hands flying over her console to silence the alarm. **"We're venting atmosphere from the port cargo hold! The internal blast doors have sealed, but the structural integrity is compromised!"** She tries to run a remote diagnostic, but the readings are a chaotic mess. **"I can't get a clear reading from here. The impact must have damaged the local sensors."**

She unstraps herself, her expression a mask of grim determination. **"I have to patch it from the inside. We can't risk a jump with an open wound like that."** She rises unsteadily, bracing herself against the lurching of the ship, and heads for the cockpit door. **"Keep her steady, Wes. I'm going to the forward hold."**

**”Kriff!”** Wes exclaimed as he felt the gravimetric forces buffet the *Moon*.   And unexpected *shove* as they were passing through the doors sent the ship skidding to the port side.   The deafening screeching sound brought forth more cursing as he righted the ship as the control board lit up with multiple red warning alarms.    He hazard a side-long glance at Marina.   Her hands were furiously moving across her station.  What she said next confirmed his fears.  They and a hull breach.  


As Marina started to unbuckle herself she explained what she needed to do.  **”Understood.  Roric, go with her.  I have things up here.  If that breach is bad an extra pair of hands will help.”**  His voice held an edge to it.  A byproduct of nearly getting them killed before they were even away from the *Veiled Shadow.*  He stole a quick glance at Marina and then Roric as he silenced another alarm.   **”Be careful.”**  He didn’t wait for a response as another alarm sounded off.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: **"Copy, Wes,"** Roric grunts, already unstrapping himself from his seat. He grabs a heavy-duty emergency patch kit from a nearby locker, his movements economical and sure. **"I'm with you, Cloudspinner. Let's go seal that wound."**

Wes fights the controls, his hands a blur of practiced motion as he wrestles with the wounded Ghtroc. With a final, powerful thrust of the maneuvering jets, he breaks free of the gravitic eddy's pull, the ship finally settling into a stable, if slightly listing, hover in the debris-strewn space just outside the *Veiled Shadow*. The cacophony of alarms on his console silences, leaving only a single, steady red light indicating the breach in the forward hold.

A few minutes pass in tense silence, broken only by the distant sounds of Marina and Roric working. Then, Marina's voice, strained but triumphant, comes over the comms.

**"Wes, we've got it. It's not pretty, but the breach is sealed. We used a cold-weld patch; it'll hold for the jump, but it's a temporary fix. That whole mandible section will need a full structural overhaul when we get back to the *Chu'unthor*."** The relief in her voice is palpable. **"We're heading back to the cockpit."**

Tense seconds stretched into minutes.  In that time he had managed to get the ship outside and in a somewhat stable position.  He shook his head knowing that he should have compensated for the gravimetric disturbances that were going to happen.  


A moment later Marina’s voice came over the comms.   The patch was in place.  It wasn’t pretty but it would hold.  **”Copy.  I’ll hold us tight until you two get back up here.”**


————


With Marina and Roric strapped back into their seats.  Wes opened up the ship's comms.  **”*Void Skimmer* this is *Seventh Moon.*  We are on our way to the rendezvous point.  We got a little beat up on the way out.  Hope you have those nav coordinates.”**   As he spoke he added power to the sublight drives and made way to the meet up coordinates.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content:
The *Seventh Moon*, its port mandible bearing a fresh, ugly scar of welded plasteel, banks slowly away from the colossal, silent form of the *Veiled Shadow*. Wes expertly guides the freighter through the treacherous Graveyard Shallows, its ancient engines humming a steady, reliable rhythm.

After a few tense minutes of navigating the debris field, a familiar, angular shape resolves on their forward sensors, growing steadily larger: the *Void Skimmer*.

Kael's voice, clipped and professional, crackles over the comm, a welcome sound of normalcy after the chaos. **"Solid copy, *Seventh Moon*. We read you. We've been holding at the rally point and monitoring your... noisy departure. Good to see you in one piece, mostly."** There's a beat of silence. **"Nav-coordinates for home are locked and ready to transmit on your mark. How copy, Wes?"**

Wes shook his head at Kael’s ribbing.   His fingers moved across the controls as he moved closer to the other half of his team.  Pulling up a respectable distance away he mimicked the trajectory Kael had the *Void Skimmer* facing.  


**”Copy that *Skimmer.*  Mostly in one piece.  We’re good to get home but no further.  Ready to receive the coordinates.”**  He waited for a few seconds as the coordinates were received.   He looked over at Marina as she entered the info into the nav computer.   A moment later she gave him a nod as she went back to looking at her station.  He looked at the reading on the hyperdrive.   


**”Nav coordinates are entered and look good.  Thanks *Skimmer.*  We’ll follow on a two second delay.  See you at the Chu’unthor.”**  He set the *Moon* to jump two seconds after the *Skimmer.*  


He looked over at Marina.   He smiled beneath his helmet and gave her a small nod.    He looked back out the view port to see the *Skimmer* make the jump.   **”Here we go.”** He said to his crew.

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: **"Acknowledged, *Seventh Moon*,"** Kael's voice crackles back. **"Transmitting Korta's return route now. Initiating jump sequence on my mark. Three... two... one..."**

Through the Ghtroc's viewport, Wes and the others watch as the *Void Skimmer* elongates and then vanishes in a blinding flash of light, plunging into hyperspace. Two seconds later, Wes engages his own hyperdrive. The ancient engines of the *Seventh Moon* roar, the ship shudders for a moment, and then the star-dusted chaos of the Graveyard Shallows is replaced by the familiar, streaking blue-white lines of hyperspace. They are on their way home.

The ship settles into the steady, monotonous hum of faster-than-light travel. The immediate dangers are behind them, leaving the small crew in the quiet, contained world of the Ghtroc's cockpit. Roric, seeing no immediate need for security, gives a grunt and heads back into the main hold to find a place to rest.
**"Well,"** Wes says, the tension finally leaving his voice as he eases back in the pilot's seat. **"So far, so good. She's holding together."**

author.username: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3
author.global_name: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

userName: Wes Del-Fin ●  HP▕▆ 76/92; CT 0 • FP 7; DP 3

content: Marina lets out a long, slow breath, a tired but deeply satisfied smile on her face. **"She's a good ship. Just needed a little... encouragement."** She finishes monitoring the last of the post-jump diagnostics, all of which are blessedly green. With the *Seventh Moon* on a stable course through Korta's Passage, she turns in her co-pilot's chair to face Wes, the close confines of the cockpit creating a private, intimate space. **"We did it, Wes. We actually did it."** Her eyes are bright, reflecting the star-streams of hyperspace and the profound relief and pride she feels. The chaos and danger of the last few hours seem to melt away, leaving just the two of them, together, heading home with a prize far greater than they could have imagined.

As Marina spoke affectionately of the *Moon* he reached up to remove his helmet.   He then set it on the far left side of his station.  Looking at the area he was going to need a better place to put it.   He smiled at the thought that maybe the council would let them keep the ship to use for missions.   Not to mention come back to finish what he said he would.


He turned his chair to face Marina.   She looked relieved and happy that they were now safely away.   Within the Force he could also feel the physical tiredness.  They had just finished a tough mission and she did it pregnant.  It just went to show him how strong she was.
